text.NRSV: An account of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: The Lineage Roll of Jesus Christ,—Son of David, Son of Abraham.

text.NRSV: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
text.Rotherham: Abraham begat Isaac, and Isaac begat Jacob, and Jacob, begat Judah and his brethren;

text.NRSV: and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Aram,
text.Rotherham: And Judah begat Perez and Zarah of Tamah, and Perez begat Hezron, and Hezron begat Ram;

text.NRSV: and Aram the father of Aminadab, and Aminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
text.Rotherham: And Ram begat Amminadab, and Amminadab begat Nashon, and Nashon begat Salmon;

text.NRSV: and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
text.Rotherham: And Salmon begat Boaz of Rahab, and Boaz begat Obed of Ruth, and Obed begat Jesse;

text.NRSV: and Jesse the father of King David. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
text.Rotherham: And Jesse begat David the King. And David begat Solomon of her [that had been the wife] of Uriah;

text.NRSV: and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
text.Rotherham: And Solomon begat Rehoboam, and Rehoboam begat Abijah, and Abijah begat Asa;

text.NRSV: and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
text.Rotherham: And Asa begat Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat begat Joram, and Joram begat Uzziah;

text.NRSV: and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
text.Rotherham: And Uzziah begat Jotham, and Jotham begat Ahaz, and Ahaz begat Hezekiah:

text.NRSV: and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos, and Amos the father of Josiah,
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah begat Manasseh, and Manasseh begat Amon, and Amon begat Josiah;

text.NRSV: and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: And Josiah begat Jechoniah, and his brethren,—upon the removal to Babylon.

text.NRSV: And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Salathiel, and Salathiel the father of Zerubbabel,
text.Rotherham: And, after the removal to Babylon, Jechoniah begat Shealtiel, and Shealtiel begat Zerubbabel;

text.NRSV: and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
text.Rotherham: And Zerubbabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begat Azor;

text.NRSV: and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
text.Rotherham: And Azor begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud;

text.NRSV: and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
text.Rotherham: And Eliud begat Eleazar, and Eleazar begat Matthan, and Matthan begat Jacob;—

text.NRSV: and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob begat Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus,—who is called Christ.

text.NRSV: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
text.Rotherham: So then, all the generations from Abraham unto David, are, fourteen, generations, and, from David unto the removal to Babylon, fourteen, generations; and, from the removal to Babylon unto the Christ, fourteen, generations.

text.NRSV: Now the birth of Jesus the Messiah took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been engaged to Joseph, but before they lived together, she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Now the birth, of [Jesus] Christ, was, thus: His mother Mary having been betrothed to Joseph,—before they came together, she was found with child by [the] Holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: Her husband Joseph, being a righteous man and unwilling to expose her to public disgrace, planned to dismiss her quietly.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Joseph her husband, being, righteous, and yet unwilling to expose her,—intended, privately, to divorce her.

text.NRSV: But just when he had resolved to do this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: But, when, these things, he had pondered, lo! a messenger of the Lord, by dream, appeared to him, saying,—Joseph, son of David! do not fear to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for, that which, in her, hath been begotten, is of [the], Holy, Spirit.

text.NRSV: She will bear a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus,—for, he, will save his people from their sins.

text.NRSV: All this took place to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
text.Rotherham: But, all this, hath come to pass, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord, through the prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “Look, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall name him Emmanuel,” which means, “God is with us.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! a Virgin, shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son,—and they shall call his name Emmanuel; which is, being translated, God with us.

text.NRSV: When Joseph awoke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him; he took her as his wife,
text.Rotherham: And Joseph, awaking, from his sleep, did as the messenger of the Lord directed him,—and took unto him his wife;

text.NRSV: but had no marital relations with her until she had borne a son; and he named him Jesus.
text.Rotherham: and knew her not, until she had brought forth a son,—and he called his name Jesus.

text.NRSV: In the time of King Herod, after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: Now, when, Jesus, was born in Bethlehem of Judaea, in the days of Herod the king, lo! wise men from eastern parts, came into Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: asking, “Where is the child who has been born king of the Jews? For we observed his star at its rising, and have come to pay him homage.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Where is he that hath been born king of the Jews? for we saw his star in the east, and have come to bow down to him.

text.NRSV: When King Herod heard this, he was frightened, and all Jerusalem with him;
text.Rotherham: But, when King Herod heard, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him;

text.NRSV: and calling together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
text.Rotherham: and, assembling all the high-priests and scribes of the people, he was enquiring of them—Where is, the Christ, to be born?

text.NRSV: They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for so it has been written by the prophet:
text.Rotherham: And, they, said to him, In Bethlehem of Judaea,—for, so, is it written through the prophet:

text.NRSV: ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means least, art thou, among the governors of Judah,—For, out of thee, shall one come forth to govern, Who shall shepherd my people Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Herod secretly called for the wise men and learned from them the exact time when the star had appeared.
text.Rotherham: Then Herod, privately, calling the wise men, ascertained from them the time of the appearing star;

text.NRSV: Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, “Go and search diligently for the child; and when you have found him, bring me word so that I may also go and pay him homage.”
text.Rotherham: and sending them to Bethlehem, said—Go search out accurately concerning the child,—and, as soon as ye find it, bring report unto me, that, I also, may come and bow myself down to it.

text.NRSV: When they had heard the king, they set out; and there, ahead of them, went the star that they had seen at its rising, until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text.Rotherham: Now, they, hearing the king, went, and lo! the star which they had seen in the east, was going before them, until it went and stood over where the child was;

text.NRSV: When they saw that the star had stopped, they were overwhelmed with joy.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy;

text.NRSV: On entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother; and they knelt down and paid him homage. Then, opening their treasure chests, they offered him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
text.Rotherham: and, coming into the house, they saw the child, with Mary its mother,—and, falling down, prostrated themselves unto it, and, opening their treasures, offered unto it gifts—gold and frankincense and myrrh;

text.NRSV: And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they left for their own country by another road.
text.Rotherham: and, being instructed by dream, not to return unto Herod, by another way, retired they unto their own country.

text.NRSV: Now after they had left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when they had retired, lo! a messenger of the Lord, appeareth by dream unto Joseph, saying, Arise, take unto thee the child and its mother, and flee into Egypt, and be there, until I tell thee,—for Herod is about to seek the child, to destroy it.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother by night, and went to Egypt,
text.Rotherham: And, he, arising, took unto him the child and its mother, by night, and retired into Egypt;

text.NRSV: and remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet, “Out of Egypt I have called my son.”
text.Rotherham: and was there, until the death of Herod,—that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt, called I my son.

text.NRSV: When Herod saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, he was infuriated, and he sent and killed all the children in and around Bethlehem who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had learned from the wise men.
text.Rotherham: Then Herod, seeing that he had been mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly enraged,—and sent and slew all the male children that were in Bethlehem, and in all its bounds, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had ascertained from the wise men.

text.NRSV: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken through the prophet Jeremiah:
text.Rotherham: Then was fulfilled, that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “A voice was heard in Ramah, wailing and loud lamentation, Rachel weeping for her children; she refused to be consoled, because they are no more.”
text.Rotherham: A voice, in Ramah, was heard, weeping and great mourning,—Rachel weeping for her children, and would not be comforted,—because they are not.

text.NRSV: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord suddenly appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt and said,
text.Rotherham: But, when Herod was, dead, lo! a messenger of the Lord, appeareth by dream unto Joseph in Egypt;

text.NRSV: “Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were seeking the child’s life are dead.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Arise! and take unto thee the child and its mother, and be journeying into the land of Israel,—for they are dead, who were seeking the life of the child.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, he, arising, took unto him the child and its mother, and entered into the land of Israel.

text.NRSV: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And after being warned in a dream, he went away to the district of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: Hearing, however, that, Archelaus, was reigning over Judaea instead of his father Herod, he was afraid, thither, to go,—and so, being instructed by dream, he retired into the parts of Galilee;

text.NRSV: There he made his home in a town called Nazareth, so that what had been spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled, “He will be called a Nazorean.”
text.Rotherham: and came and fixed his dwelling in a city called Nazareth,—that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets—A Nazarene, shall he be called.

text.NRSV: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness of Judea, proclaiming,
text.Rotherham: Now, in those days, came John the Immerser, proclaiming in the wilderness of Judaea;

text.NRSV: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
text.Rotherham: saying, Repent ye,—for the kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near.

text.NRSV: This is the one of whom the prophet Isaiah spoke when he said, “The voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.’”
text.Rotherham: For, this, is he who was spoken of through Isaiah the prophet, saying, A voice, of one crying aloud! In the wilderness, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths.

text.NRSV: Now John wore clothing of camel’s hair with a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
text.Rotherham: But John, himself, had his raiment of camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins,—while, his food, was locusts and wild honey.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Jerusalem and all Judea were going out to him, and all the region along the Jordan,
text.Rotherham: Then, were going forth unto him—Jerusalem, and all Judaea, and all the country round about the Jordan:

text.NRSV: and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.Rotherham: and were being immersed in the Jordan river, by him, openly confessing their sins.

text.NRSV: But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
text.Rotherham: But, seeing, many of the Pharisees and Sadducees, coming unto his immersion, he said to them,—Broods of vipers! who suggested to you, to be fleeing from the coming wrath?

text.NRSV: Bear fruit worthy of repentance.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth, therefore, fruit worthy of repentance;

text.NRSV: Do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our ancestor’; for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.
text.Rotherham: and think not to be saying within yourselves,—As our father, we have, Abraham; for, I say unto you, that God is able, out of these stones, to raise up children unto Abraham.

text.NRSV: Even now the ax is lying at the root of the trees; every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.Rotherham: Already also, the axe, unto the root of the trees, is being laid,—every tree, therefore, not bringing forth good fruit, is to be hewn down, and, into fire, to be cast.

text.NRSV: “I baptize you with water for repentance, but one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
text.Rotherham: I, indeed, am immersing you, in water, unto repentance,—but, he who, after me, cometh is, mightier than I, whose, sandals, I am not worthy to bear, he, will immerse you, in Holy Spirit and fire:

text.NRSV: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and will gather his wheat into the granary; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
text.Rotherham: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will clear out his threshing-floor,—and will gather his wheat into the granary, but, the chaff, will he burn up with fire unquenchable.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan, to be baptized by him.
text.Rotherham: Then, cometh Jesus, from Galilee to the Jordan, unto John,—to be immersed by him.

text.NRSV: John would have prevented him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, would have hindered him, saying—I, have, need, by thee, to be immersed,—and dost, thou, come unto me?

text.NRSV: But Jesus answered him, “Let it be so now; for it is proper for us in this way to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he consented.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus answering, said unto him, Suffer [me] even now,—for, thus, it becometh us, to fulfil, all righteousness; then, he suffered him.

text.NRSV: And when Jesus had been baptized, just as he came up from the water, suddenly the heavens were opened to him and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, having been immersed, straightway, went up from the water,—and lo! the heavens were opened and he saw the Spirit of God, descending like a dove coming upon him;

text.NRSV: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, the Beloved, with whom I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and lo! a voice out of the heavens,—saying, This, is my Son, the Beloved, in whom I delight.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus was led up into the wilderness, by the Spirit, to be tempted by the adversary;

text.NRSV: He fasted forty days and forty nights, and afterwards he was famished.
text.Rotherham: and, fasting forty days and forty nights,—afterwards, he hungered.

text.NRSV: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, the tempter said to him,—If thou art God’s, Son, speak! that, these stones, may become, loaves.

text.NRSV: But he answered, “It is written, ‘One does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said, It is written,—Not on bread alone, shall man live, but on every declaration coming forth through the mouth of God.

text.NRSV: Then the devil took him to the holy city and placed him on the pinnacle of the temple,
text.Rotherham: Then, the adversary taketh him with him, unto the holy city,—and he set him upon the pinnacle of the temple;

text.NRSV: saying to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down; for it is written, ‘He will give his angels charge over you,’ and ‘On their hands they will bear you up, so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.’”
text.Rotherham: and saith to him—If thou art God’s, Son, cast thyself down,—for it is written, To his messengers, will he give charge concerning thee; and, on hands, will they bear thee up, lest once thou strike, against a stone, thy foot.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Again it is written, ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said to him, Again, it is written,—Thou shalt not put to the test, the Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor;
text.Rotherham: Again, the adversary taketh him with him, into an exceeding high mountain,—and pointeth out to him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory;

text.NRSV: and he said to him, “All these I will give you, if you will fall down and worship me.”
text.Rotherham: and said to him, All these things, will I give thee,—if thou wilt fall down and worship me.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Away with you, Satan! for it is written, ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve only him.’”
text.Rotherham: Then saith Jesus unto him, Withdraw, Satan! for it is written,—The Lord thy God, shalt thou worship, and, to him alone, render divine service.

text.NRSV: Then the devil left him, and suddenly angels came and waited on him.
text.Rotherham: Then, the adversary leaveth him,—and lo! messengers came near, and began ministering unto him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing that, John, had been delivered up, he retired into Galilee;

text.NRSV: He left Nazareth and made his home in Capernaum by the sea, in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali,
text.Rotherham: and, forsaking Nazareth, he came and fixed his dwelling in Capernaum, which was by the lake,—within the bounds of Zebulun and Naphtali:

text.NRSV: so that what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled:
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “Land of Zebulun, land of Naphtali, on the road by the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles—
text.Rotherham: Land of Zebulun, and land of Naphtali, the lake-way across the Jordan,—Galilee of the nations,

text.NRSV: the people who sat in darkness have seen a great light, and for those who sat in the region and shadow of death light has dawned.”
text.Rotherham: The people that was sitting in darkness, a great light, beheld,—and, on them who were sitting in land and shade of death, Light rose on them.

text.NRSV: From that time Jesus began to proclaim, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
text.Rotherham: From that time, began Jesus to be making proclamation, and saying,—Repent ye, for the kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near.

text.NRSV: As he walked by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea—for they were fishermen.
text.Rotherham: And, walking round beside the sea of Galilee, he saw two brethren—Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,—casting a large fishing-net into the sea, for they were fishers;

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fish for people.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them, Come after me,—and I will make you fishers, of men.

text.NRSV: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, straightway, leaving the nets, followed him.

text.NRSV: As he went from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John, in the boat with their father Zebedee, mending their nets, and he called them.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward from thence, he saw other two brethren—James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother—in the boat with Zebedee their father, putting in order their nets,—and he called them.

text.NRSV: Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, straightway, leaving the boat and their father, followed him.

text.NRSV: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom and curing every disease and every sickness among the people.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was going round throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the glad-message of the kingdom,—and curing every disease, and every infirmity, among the people.

text.NRSV: So his fame spread throughout all Syria, and they brought to him all the sick, those who were afflicted with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, and paralytics, and he cured them.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the report of him into all Syria; and they brought unto him all who were sick, with divers diseases and tortures, distressed, demonized and lunatic and paralyzed,—and he cured them.

text.NRSV: And great crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And there followed him large multitudes—from Galilee, and Decapolis, and Jerusalem, and Judaea, and across the Jordan.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up the mountain; and after he sat down, his disciples came to him.
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain,—and, when he had taken a seat, his disciples came unto him;

text.NRSV: Then he began to speak, and taught them, saying:
text.Rotherham: and, opening his mouth, he began teaching them, saying:—

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the destitute, in spirit; for, theirs, is the kingdom of the heavens;

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who mourn; for, they, shall be comforted:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the meek; for, they, shall inherit the earth:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who hunger and thirst for righteousness; for, they, shall be filled:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the merciful, for they will receive mercy.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the merciful; for, they, shall receive mercy:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the pure, in heart; for, they, shall, see God:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the peacemakers; for, they, shall be, called sons of God:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness’ sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who have been persecuted for righteousness’ sake; for, theirs, is the kingdom of the heavens.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you when people revile you and persecute you and utter all kinds of evil against you falsely on my account.
text.Rotherham: Happy, are ye, whensoever they may reproach you and persecute you, and say every evil thing against you, falsely, for my sake:

text.NRSV: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice and exult, because, your reward, is great in the heavens; for, so, persecuted they the prophets who were before you.

text.NRSV: “You are the salt of the earth; but if salt has lost its taste, how can its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything, but is thrown out and trampled under foot.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are the salt of the earth; but, if the salt become tasteless, wherewith shall it be salted? it is good, for nothing, any more, save, being cast out, to be trampled on by men.

text.NRSV: “You are the light of the world. A city built on a hill cannot be hid.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are the light of the world: it is impossible for a city to be hid, on the top of a mountain, lying.

text.NRSV: No one after lighting a lamp puts it under the bushel basket, but on the lampstand, and it gives light to all in the house.
text.Rotherham: Neither light they a lamp, and place it under the measure; but upon the lampstand, and it giveth light to all that are in the house.

text.NRSV: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: In like manner, let your light shine before men,—that they may see your good works,—and glorify your Father who is in the heavens.

text.NRSV: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the law or the prophets; I have come not to abolish but to fulfill.
text.Rotherham: Do not think, that I came to pull down the law, or the prophets,—I came not to pull down, but to fulfil.

text.NRSV: For truly I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not one letter, not one stroke of a letter, will pass from the law until all is accomplished.
text.Rotherham: For, verily, I say unto you, until the heaven and the earth shall pass away, one least letter, or one point, may in nowise pass away from the law, till all be accomplished.

text.NRSV: Therefore, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others to do the same, will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever, therefore, shall relax one of these commandments, the least, and teach men so, shall be called, least, in the kingdom of the heavens; but, whosoever shall do and teach, the same, shall be called, great, in the kingdom of the heavens.

text.NRSV: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: For I say unto you, that, unless your righteousness exceed that of the Scribes and Pharisees, in nowise, may ye enter into the kingdom of the heavens.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not murder’; and ‘whoever murders shall be liable to judgment.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, to them of olden time,—Thou shalt not commit murder, and, whosoever shall commit murder, shall be, liable, to judgment.

text.NRSV: But I say to you that if you are angry with a brother or sister, you will be liable to judgment; and if you insult a brother or sister, you will be liable to the council; and if you say, ‘You fool,’ you will be liable to the hell of fire.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, every one who is angry with his brother, shall be, liable, to judgment,—and, whosoever shall say to his brother, Worthless one!, shall be, liable, to the high council; and, whosoever shall say, Rebel!, shall be, liable, unto the fiery gehenna.

text.NRSV: So when you are offering your gift at the altar, if you remember that your brother or sister has something against you,
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, thou be bearing thy gift towards the altar, and, there, shouldst remember that, thy brother, hath aught against thee,

text.NRSV: leave your gift there before the altar and go; first be reconciled to your brother or sister, and then come and offer your gift.
text.Rotherham: leave, there, thy gift before the altar, and withdraw,—first, be reconciled unto thy brother, and, then, coming, be offering thy gift.

text.NRSV: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are on the way to court with him, or your accuser may hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you will be thrown into prison.
text.Rotherham: Be making agreement with thine adversary, quickly, while thou art with him, in the way,—lest once thine adversary deliver thee up unto the judge, and the judge, unto the officer, and, into prison, thou be cast.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto thee, In nowise, mayest thou come out from thence, until thou pay the last halfpenny.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, Thou shalt not commit adultery:

text.NRSV: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, Every one who looketh on a woman so as to covet her, already, hath committed adultery with her, in his heart.

text.NRSV: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
text.Rotherham: And, if, thy right eye, is causing thee to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from thee,—for it profiteth thee, that, one of thy members, should perish, and not, thy whole body, be cast into gehenna.

text.NRSV: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to go into hell.
text.Rotherham: And, if, thy right hand, is causing thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from thee,—for it profiteth thee, that, one of thy members, should perish, and not, thy whole body, into gehenna, depart.

text.NRSV: “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
text.Rotherham: It was said, moreover, Whosoever shall divorce his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement;

text.NRSV: But I say to you that anyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of unchastity, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, Everyone who divorceth his wife—saving for unfaithfulness, causeth her to be made an adulteress,—[and, whosoever shall marry a divorced woman, committeth adultery].

text.NRSV: “Again, you have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not swear falsely, but carry out the vows you have made to the Lord.’
text.Rotherham: Again, ye have heard that it was said, to them of olden time, Thou shalt not swear falsely,—but shalt render unto the Lord, thine oaths.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Do not swear at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
text.Rotherham: But, I, tell you—not to swear, at all: Neither by heaven, because it is the, throne of God,—

text.NRSV: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
text.Rotherham: Nor by the earth, because it is his, footstool; nor by Jerusalem, because, it is the, city, of the Great King;

text.NRSV: And do not swear by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
text.Rotherham: Nor, by thine own head, mayest thou swear, because thou art not able to make, one hair, white or black.

text.NRSV: Let your word be ‘Yes, Yes’ or ‘No, No’; anything more than this comes from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: But let your word be, Yea, yea,—Nay, nay; and, what goeth beyond these, is, of evil.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said,—Eye for eye, and tooth for tooth.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Do not resist an evildoer. But if anyone strikes you on the right cheek, turn the other also;
text.Rotherham: But, I, tell you, not to resist evil,—Nay, whoever is smiting thee on the right cheek, turn to him, the other also;

text.NRSV: and if anyone wants to sue you and take your coat, give your cloak as well;
text.Rotherham: And, him who is desiring thee to be judged, and to take, thy tunic, let him have, thy mantle also.

text.NRSV: and if anyone forces you to go one mile, go also the second mile.
text.Rotherham: And, whoever shall impress thee one mile, go with him two:

text.NRSV: Give to everyone who begs from you, and do not refuse anyone who wants to borrow from you.
text.Rotherham: To him who is asking thee, give; and, him who is desiring from thee to borrow, do not thou turn away.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, Be loving your enemies, and praying for them who are persecuting you:

text.NRSV: so that you may be children of your Father in heaven; for he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the righteous and on the unrighteous.
text.Rotherham: That ye may become sons of your Father who is in the heavens: because, his sun, he maketh arise on evil and good, and sendeth rain, on just and unjust.

text.NRSV: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
text.Rotherham: For, if ye love them that love you, what reward have ye? are not, even the tax-collectors, the same thing, doing?

text.NRSV: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
text.Rotherham: And, if ye salute your brethren only, what, more than common, are ye doing? are not, even the nations, the same thing, doing?

text.NRSV: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, shall become, perfect: as, your heavenly Father, is perfect.

text.NRSV: “Beware of practicing your piety before others in order to be seen by them; for then you have no reward from your Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: [But] take heed, that ye do not, your righteousness, before men, to be gazed at by them,—otherwise at least, reward, have ye none, with your Father who is in the heavens.

text.NRSV: “So whenever you give alms, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may be praised by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, thou mayest be doing an alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, just as, the hypocrites, do in the synagogues and in the streets—that they may be glorified by men,—Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.

text.NRSV: But when you give alms, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
text.Rotherham: But, thou, when doing an alms, let not, thy left hand, know what thy right hand is doing;

text.NRSV: so that your alms may be done in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: that thine alms may be in secret,—and, thy Father, who seeth in secret, will give it back to thee.

text.NRSV: “And whenever you pray, do not be like the hypocrites; for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners, so that they may be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye may be praying, ye shall not be as the hypocrites, because they love, in the synagogues, and at the corners of the broad ways, to take their stand and pray, that they may shine before men; Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.

text.NRSV: But whenever you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, when thou wouldest pray, enter into thy closet, and, fastening thy door, pray unto thy Father who is in secret,—and, thy Father who seeth in secret, will reward thee.

text.NRSV: “When you are praying, do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do; for they think that they will be heard because of their many words.
text.Rotherham: And, being at prayer, use not vain repetitions, just like the nations,—for they think, that, in their much speaking, they shall be hoard;

text.NRSV: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
text.Rotherham: do not, therefore, make yourselves like them, for [God] your Father knoweth of what things ye have, need, before ye ask him.

text.NRSV: “Pray then in this way: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name.
text.Rotherham: Thus, therefore pray, ye: Our Father, who art in the heavens,—Hallowed be thy name,

text.NRSV: Your kingdom come. Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Come may thy kingdom,—Accomplished be thy will, as in heaven, also on earth:

text.NRSV: Give us this day our daily bread.
text.Rotherham: Our needful bread, give us, this day;

text.NRSV: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
text.Rotherham: And forgive us our debts, as, we also, have forgiven our debtors;

text.NRSV: And do not bring us to the time of trial, but rescue us from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: And bring us not into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.

text.NRSV: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you;
text.Rotherham: For, if ye forgive men their faults, Your Father who is in the heavens, will forgive, even you;

text.NRSV: but if you do not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
text.Rotherham: But, if ye forgive not men [their faults], neither will your Father forgive, your faults.

text.NRSV: “And whenever you fast, do not look dismal, like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces so as to show others that they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye may he fasting, become not ye, as the hypocrites, of sullen countenance,—for they darken their looks, that they may appear, unto men, to be fasting: Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.

text.NRSV: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
text.Rotherham: But, when, thou, art fasting, anoint thy head, and, thy face, wash,—

text.NRSV: so that your fasting may be seen not by others but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: that thou do not appear, unto men, to be fasting, but to thy Father who is in secret,—and, thy Father who seeth in secret, will reward thee.

text.NRSV: “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust consume and where thieves break in and steal;
text.Rotherham: Be not laying up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where, moth and rust, do tarnish, and where, thieves, dig through and steal;

text.NRSV: but store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consumes and where thieves do not break in and steal.
text.Rotherham: but be laying up or yourselves treasures in heaven, where, neither moth nor rust, doth tarnish, and where, thieves, dig not through nor steal:

text.NRSV: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
text.Rotherham: for, where, thy treasure, is, there, will be, thy heart [also].

text.NRSV: “The eye is the lamp of the body. So, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light;
text.Rotherham: The lamp of the body, is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body, shall be, lighted up;

text.NRSV: but if your eye is unhealthy, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
text.Rotherham: But, if thine eye be, evil, thy whole body, shall be, in the dark;—if therefore, the light which is in thee, is, darkness, the darkness, how great!

text.NRSV: “No one can serve two masters; for a slave will either hate the one and love the other, or be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
text.Rotherham: No one, unto two masters, can be in service; for either, the one, he will hate, and, the other ,love, or, one, he will hold to, and, the other, despise: Ye cannot be in service, unto God, and, unto Riches.

text.NRSV: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I say unto you: Be not anxious for your life, what ye shall eat [or what ye shall drink],—or for your body, what ye shall put on: Is not, the life, more than, the food? And, the body, than, the raiment?

text.NRSV: Look at the birds of the air; they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
text.Rotherham: Observe intently, the birds of the heaven,—that they neither sow, nor reap, nor gather into barns, and yet, your heavenly Father, feedeth, them: Are no, ye, much better than, they?

text.NRSV: And can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your span of life?
text.Rotherham: But who from among you, being anxious, can add to his stature one cubit?

text.NRSV: And why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they neither toil nor spin,
text.Rotherham: And, about clothing, why are ye anxious? Consider well the lilies of the field, how they grow,—they toil not neither do they spin;

text.NRSV: yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not clothed like one of these.
text.Rotherham: And yet, I say unto you, not even Solomon, in all his glory, was arrayed like, one of these!

text.NRSV: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is alive today and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith?
text.Rotherham: Now, if the grass of the field—which to-day, is, and, to-morrow, into an oven, is cast—God thus adorneth, not much rather, you, little of faith?

text.NRSV: Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What will we eat?’ or ‘What will we drink?’ or ‘What will we wear?’
text.Rotherham: Do not then be anxious saying, What shall we eat? or What shall we drink? or Wherewithal shall we be arrayed?

text.NRSV: For it is the Gentiles who strive for all these things; and indeed your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
text.Rotherham: For, all these things, the nations, seek after,—for your heavenly Father, knoweth, that ye are needing, all these things.

text.NRSV: But strive first for the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
text.Rotherham: But be seeking first, the kingdom and its righteousness,—and, all these things, shall be added unto you.

text.NRSV: “So do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will bring worries of its own. Today’s trouble is enough for today.
text.Rotherham: Do not, then, be anxious for the morrow; for the morrow, will be anxious, for itself: Sufficient for the day, is the evil thereof.

text.NRSV: “Do not judge, so that you may not be judged.
text.Rotherham: Judge not, that ye be not judged;—

text.NRSV: For with the judgment you make you will be judged, and the measure you give will be the measure you get.
text.Rotherham: For, with what judgment ye judge, shall ye be judged,—and, with what measure ye mete, shall it be measured unto you.

text.NRSV: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: Why, moreover, beholdest thou the mote, in the eye of thy brother,—while, the beam in thine own eye, thou dost not consider?

text.NRSV: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while the log is in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: Or how wilt thou say unto thy brother, Let me cast the mote out of thine eye,—when lo! a beam, is in thine own eye?

text.NRSV: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
text.Rotherham: Hypocrite! cast first, out of thine own eye, the beam,—and, then, shalt thou see clearly to cast the mote, out of the eye of thy brother.

text.NRSV: “Do not give what is holy to dogs; and do not throw your pearls before swine, or they will trample them under foot and turn and maul you.
text.Rotherham: Do not give what is holy unto dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine,—lest once they trample, them, down with their feet, and, turning, tear, you.

text.NRSV: “Ask, and it will be given you; search, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text.Rotherham: Be asking, and it shall be given you, Be seeking, and ye shall find,—Be knocking, and it shall be opened unto you.

text.NRSV: For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who searches finds, and for everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever asketh, receiveth, and, he that seeketh, findeth,—and, to him that knocketh, shall it be opened.

text.NRSV: Is there anyone among you who, if your child asks for bread, will give a stone?
text.Rotherham: Or what man, from among yourselves, whom his son shall ask for a loaf,—a stone, will give him?

text.NRSV: Or if the child asks for a fish, will give a snake?
text.Rotherham: Or, a fish also, shall ask,—a serpent, will give him?

text.NRSV: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
text.Rotherham: If then, ye, being, evil, know how, good gifts, to be giving unto your children, how much more, will, your Father who is in the heavens, give good things to them that ask, him?

text.NRSV: “In everything do to others as you would have them do to you; for this is the law and the prophets.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever things, therefore, ye would, that men be doing unto you, so, be, ye also, doing, unto them,—for, this, is the law and the prophets.

text.NRSV: “Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the road is easy that leads to destruction, and there are many who take it.
text.Rotherham: Enter ye in at, the narrow gate; because broad and roomy is the way that leadeth unto destruction,—and, many, are they who enter thereby:

text.NRSV: For the gate is narrow and the road is hard that leads to life, and there are few who find it.
text.Rotherham: Because, narrow, is the gate, and, confined, the way, that leadeth unto life,—and, few, are they who find, it.

text.NRSV: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
text.Rotherham: Beware of false prophets, who come unto you in clothing of sheep,—while, within, they are ravening wolves.

text.NRSV: You will know them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thorns, or figs from thistles?
text.Rotherham: By their fruits, shall ye find, them, out,—unless perhaps men gather—from thorns, grapes! or, from thistles, figs!

text.NRSV: In the same way, every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
text.Rotherham: So, every good tree, fine fruit, produceth,—whereas, the worthless tree, evil fruit, produceth:

text.NRSV: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
text.Rotherham: It is, impossible, for a, good tree, to be bearing, evil fruit, neither doth, a worthless tree, produce, fine fruit.

text.NRSV: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.Rotherham: Every tree that beareth not fine fruit, is hewn down, and, into fire, is cast.

text.NRSV: Thus you will know them by their fruits.
text.Rotherham: After all then, by their fruits, shall ye find, them, out.

text.NRSV: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord! Lord! shall enter into the kingdom of the heavens,—but he that doeth the will of my Father who is in the heavens.

text.NRSV: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many deeds of power in your name?’
text.Rotherham: Many, will say unto me, in, that, day, Lord! Lord! did we not, in thy name, prophesy, and, in thy name, cast, demons, out,—and, in thy name, many works of power, perform?

text.NRSV: Then I will declare to them, ‘I never knew you; go away from me, you evildoers.’
text.Rotherham: And, then, will I confess unto them, Never, have I acknowledged you,—Depart from me, ye workers of lawlessness!

text.NRSV: “Everyone then who hears these words of mine and acts on them will be like a wise man who built his house on rock.
text.Rotherham: Every one, therefore, who heareth [these] my words, and doeth them, shall be likened to a prudent man, who built his house upon the rock;

text.NRSV: The rain fell, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on rock.
text.Rotherham: And the rain descended, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and rushed against that house, and it fell not; for it had been founded upon the rock.

text.NRSV: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not act on them will be like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
text.Rotherham: And every one who heareth these my words, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand;

text.NRSV: The rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell—and great was its fall!”
text.Rotherham: And the rain descended, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and dashed against that house, and it fell; and, the fall thereof, was, great.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were astounded at his teaching,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended these words, with astonishment were the multitudes being struck at his teaching;

text.NRSV: for he taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
text.Rotherham: for he was teaching them as one having, authority, and not as their Scribes.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had come down from the mountain, great crowds followed him;
text.Rotherham: And when he came down from the mountain, there followed him large multitudes.

text.NRSV: and there was a leper who came to him and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! a leper, coming near, began to bow down to him saying,—Lord! if thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me.

text.NRSV: He stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I do choose. Be made clean!” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
text.Rotherham: And, stretching forth the hand, he touched him, saying,—I am willing, be cleansed! And, straightway, cleansed was his leprosy.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him, Mind! tell, no one,—but withdraw, thyself, shew to the priest, and offer the gift which Moses directed, for a witness unto them.

text.NRSV: When he entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, appealing to him
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered into Capernaum, there came near unto him a centurion, beseeching him,

text.NRSV: and saying, “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible distress.”
text.Rotherham: and saying, Lord! my servant, is laid prostrate in the house, a paralytic, fearfully tortured.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “I will come and cure him.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, I, will come and cure him.

text.NRSV: The centurion answered, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof; but only speak the word, and my servant will be healed.
text.Rotherham: But the centurion, answering, said, Lord! I am of no consideration, that, under my roof, thou shouldest enter,—but, only say with a word, and healed shall be my servant.

text.NRSV: For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and the slave does it.”
text.Rotherham: For, I also, am a man [set] under authority, having under myself, soldiers,—and I say to this one, Go! and he goeth, and to another, Come! and he cometh, and, to my servant, Do this! and he doeth it.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard him, he was amazed and said to those who followed him, “Truly I tell you, in no one in Israel have I found such faith.
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus, hearing, marveled, and said to them that followed,—Verily, I say unto you, With no one in Israel, such faith as this, have I found.

text.NRSV: I tell you, many will come from east and west and will eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you, Many from east and west, will have come, and shall recline with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, in the kingdom of the heavens;

text.NRSV: while the heirs of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, the sons of the kingdom, shall be cast forth into the darkness outside,—There, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: And to the centurion Jesus said, “Go; let it be done for you according to your faith.” And the servant was healed in that hour.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Withdraw! and, as thou hast believed, be it done for thee. And healed was the servant in that hour.

text.NRSV: When Jesus entered Peter’s house, he saw his mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever;
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, coming into the house of Peter, saw his mother-in-law laid prostrate and in a fever,

text.NRSV: he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she got up and began to serve him.
text.Rotherham: and he touched her hand, and the fever left her,—and she arose, and began ministering unto him.

text.NRSV: That evening they brought to him many who were possessed with demons; and he cast out the spirits with a word, and cured all who were sick.
text.Rotherham: But, when, evening, came, they brought unto him many demonized,—and he cast out the spirits with a word, and, all who were sick, he cured:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah, “He took our infirmities and bore our diseases.”
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,—Himself, our weaknesses, took, and, diseases, bare.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus saw great crowds around him, he gave orders to go over to the other side.
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus, seeing a multitude about him, gave orders to depart unto the other side.

text.NRSV: A scribe then approached and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
text.Rotherham: And a certain scribe, coming near, said unto him,—Teacher; I will follow thee, whithersoever Thou goest.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him: The foxes, have, dens, and, the birds of the heavens, nests,—but, the Son of Man, hath not where, his head he may recline.

text.NRSV: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
text.Rotherham: And, another of the disciples, saith unto him,—Lord! suffer me, first, to depart, and bury my father.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, saith unto him,—Be following me, and leave, the dead, to bury, their own dead.

text.NRSV: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, when he, entered, into a boat, his disciples followed him.

text.NRSV: A windstorm arose on the sea, so great that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but he was asleep.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a great squall, arose in the sea, so that, the boat, was being covered, by the waves,—but, he, was sleeping.

text.NRSV: And they went and woke him up, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, they aroused him, saying, Master! save, we perish!

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, you of little faith?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a dead calm.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them, Why, are ye, fearful, little of faith? Then, aroused, he rebuked the winds and the sea,—and it became a great calm.

text.NRSV: They were amazed, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?”
text.Rotherham: But, the men, marveled, saying, Whence is this one,—that, both the winds and the sea, unto him, give ear?

text.NRSV: When he came to the other side, to the country of the Gadarenes, two demoniacs coming out of the tombs met him. They were so fierce that no one could pass that way.
text.Rotherham: And, when he, came, unto the other side, into the country of the Gadarenes, there met him, two men demonized, out of the tombs, coming forth,—fierce exceedingly, so that no one could pass that way;

text.NRSV: Suddenly they shouted, “What have you to do with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
text.Rotherham: and lo! they cried aloud, saying, What have we in common with thee, O Son of God? Hast thou come hither, before the right time, to torment us?

text.NRSV: Now a large herd of swine was feeding at some distance from them.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, far from them, a herd of many swine, feeding;

text.NRSV: The demons begged him, “If you cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.”
text.Rotherham: and, the demons, began to beseech him, saying, If thou dost cast us out, send us away, into the herd of swine.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Go!” So they came out and entered the swine; and suddenly, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the water.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, Withdraw! So, they, going out, departed into the swine,—and lo! all the herd rushed down the cliff, into the sea, and died in the waters.

text.NRSV: The herdsmen ran off, and on going into the town, they told the whole story about what had happened to the demoniacs.
text.Rotherham: And, they who were feeding them, fled,—and, departing into the city, reported all things, and what concerned them who had been demonized.

text.NRSV: Then the whole town came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their neighborhood.
text.Rotherham: And lo! all the city, came out to meet Jesus,—and, seeing him, they besought that he would pass on from their bounds.

text.NRSV: And after getting into a boat he crossed the sea and came to his own town.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into a boat, he crossed over, and came into his own city.

text.NRSV: And just then some people were carrying a paralyzed man lying on a bed. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! they were bringing unto him a paralytic, on a couch, laid prostrate; and Jesus, seeing, their faith, said to the paralytic,—Take courage! child, forgiven are thy sins.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! certain of the scribes, said within themselves,—This man, speaketh profanely!

text.NRSV: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, knowing, their inward thoughts, said,—To what end are ye cherishing evil thoughts within your hearts?

text.NRSV: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
text.Rotherham: For which is easier—To say, Forgiven are thy sins,—or to say, Rise and be walking!

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he then said to the paralytic—“Get up, take your bed and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know, that the Son of Man hath, authority, upon the earth, to be forgiving sins, then, saith he to the paralytic,—Rise! take up thy couch, and withdraw unto thy house.

text.NRSV: And he got up and went to his home.
text.Rotherham: And, rising, he departed unto his house.

text.NRSV: When the crowds saw it, they were filled with awe, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to human beings.
text.Rotherham: Now the multitudes, seeing, were struck with fear, and glorified the God who had given authority, such as this, unto men.

text.NRSV: As Jesus was walking along, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, passing aside from thence, saw a man presiding over the tax-office, called, Matthew, and saith unto him,—Be following me. And, arising, he followed him.

text.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in the house, many tax collectors and sinners came and were sitting with him and his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was reclining in the house, that lo! many tax-collectors and sinners, came, and were reclining together with Jesus and his disciples.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, observing it, began to say unto his disciples,—Wherefore, with tax-collectors and sinners, doth your Teacher eat?

text.NRSV: But when he heard this, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
text.Rotherham: And, he, hearing it, said,—No need have the strong, of a physician, but they who are sick.

text.NRSV: Go and learn what this means, ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
text.Rotherham: But go ye, and learn what this meaneth,—Mercy, I desire, and not, sacrifice; For I came, not to call the righteous, but sinners.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.Rotherham: Then come near unto him the disciples of John, saying,—Wherefore do, we, and the Pharisees, fast, whereas, thy disciples, fast not?

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them, can they? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them,—Can, the sons of the bridechamber, mourn, so long as, the bridegroom, is, with them? But days will come, when the bridegroom, shall be taken from them, and, then, will they fast.

text.NRSV: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak, for the patch pulls away from the cloak, and a worse tear is made.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, no one, layeth on a patch of unshrunk cloth, upon an old garment,—for the shrinking of it teareth away from the garment, and, a worse rent, is made.

text.NRSV: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins; otherwise, the skins burst, and the wine is spilled, and the skins are destroyed; but new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
text.Rotherham: Neither pour they new wine into old skins: otherwise at least, the skins are burst, and, the wine, runneth out, and, the skins, are spoiled,—but they pour new wine into unused skins, and, both, are together preserved.

text.NRSV: While he was saying these things to them, suddenly a leader of the synagogue came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died; but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
text.Rotherham: While, these things, he was speaking unto them, lo! a [certain] ruler, came, and began bowing down to him, saying,—My daughter, just now died! But come, and lay thy hand upon, her, and she shall live.

text.NRSV: And Jesus got up and followed him, with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, arising, was following him, also his disciples.

text.NRSV: Then suddenly a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his cloak,
text.Rotherham: And lo! a woman, having a flow of blood twelve years, coming near behind, touched the fringe of his mantle.

text.NRSV: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be made well.”
text.Rotherham: For she kept saying within herself,—If only I touch his mantle, I shall be made well!

text.NRSV: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly the woman was made well.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, turning and seeing her, said,—Take courage! daughter, Thy faith, hath made thee well. And the woman was made well, from that hour.

text.NRSV: When Jesus came to the leader’s house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, coming, into the house of the ruler, and seeing the flute-players and the multitude in confusion, was saying:

text.NRSV: he said, “Go away; for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
text.Rotherham: Give place! for the maiden died not, but is sleeping. And they began to deride him.

text.NRSV: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
text.Rotherham: But, when the multitude had been put forth, he went in, and grasped her hand,—and the maiden arose.

text.NRSV: And the report of this spread throughout that district.
text.Rotherham: And forth went this report, into the whole of that land.

text.NRSV: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, crying loudly, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: And, as Jesus, was passing aside from thence, there followed him, two blind men, crying aloud and saying,—Have mercy on us, O Son of David!

text.NRSV: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him; and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered the house, the blind men came unto him,—and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye, that I can, do this? They say unto him, Yea, Lord!

text.NRSV: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith let it be done to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith, be it done unto you.

text.NRSV: And their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly ordered them, “See that no one knows of this.”
text.Rotherham: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus, sternly charged them, saying, Mind! let no one know!

text.NRSV: But they went away and spread the news about him throughout that district.
text.Rotherham: They, however, going forth, made him known throughout the whole of that land.

text.NRSV: After they had gone away, a demoniac who was mute was brought to him.
text.Rotherham: And, as, they, were going forth, lo! there was brought to him, a dumb man, demonized.

text.NRSV: And when the demon had been cast out, the one who had been mute spoke; and the crowds were amazed and said, “Never has anything like this been seen in Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And, the demon being cast out, the dumb man spoke; and the multitudes marvelled, saying,—Never, was it seen thus, in Israel.

text.NRSV: But the Pharisees said, “By the ruler of the demons he casts out the demons.”
text.Rotherham: [But, the Pharisees, beg to say, In the ruler of the demons, is he casting out the demons.]

text.NRSV: Then Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and curing every disease and every sickness.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was going round all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the glad-message of the kingdom,—and curing every disease, and every infirmity.

text.NRSV: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the multitudes, he was moved with compassion concerning them, because they were torn and thrown down, like sheep hating no shepherd.

text.NRSV: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto his disciples, The harvest, indeed, is great, but, the labourers, few;

text.NRSV: therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
text.Rotherham: Beg ye, therefore, of the Lord of the harvest,—That he would thrust forth labourers, into his harvest.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus summoned his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to cure every disease and every sickness.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near his twelve disciples, he gave them authority over impure spirits,—so as to be casting them out, and curing every disease and every infirmity.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, also known as Peter, and his brother Andrew; James son of Zebedee, and his brother John;
text.Rotherham: Now, the twelve apostles’ names, are these:—first Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,—and James, the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother;

text.NRSV: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
text.Rotherham: Philip, and Bartholomew,—Thomas, and Matthew, the tax-collector; James, the son of Alphaeus, and Thadaeus;

text.NRSV: Simon the Cananaean, and Judas Iscariot, the one who betrayed him.
text.Rotherham: Simon, the zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who also delivered him up.

text.NRSV: These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: “Go nowhere among the Gentiles, and enter no town of the Samaritans,
text.Rotherham: These twelve, Jesus sent forth, charging them, saying:—Into any way to the nations, do not depart,—and, into any city of Samaritans, do not enter;

text.NRSV: but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: But be going, rather, unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: As you go, proclaim the good news, ‘The kingdom of heaven has come near.’
text.Rotherham: And, as ye go, proclaim, saying, The kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near!

text.NRSV: Cure the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons. You received without payment; give without payment.
text.Rotherham: Be curing, the sick, raising, the dead,—cleansing, lepers, casting out, demons: freely, ye have, received, freely, give.

text.NRSV: Take no gold, or silver, or copper in your belts,
text.Rotherham: Ye may procure—neither gold, nor silver, nor copper, for your belts,—

text.NRSV: no bag for your journey, or two tunics, or sandals, or a staff; for laborers deserve their food.
text.Rotherham: neither satchel for journey, nor two tunics, nor sandals, nor staff; for, worthy, is the labourer, of his maintenance.

text.NRSV: Whatever town or village you enter, find out who in it is worthy, and stay there until you leave.
text.Rotherham: And, into whatsoever city or village ye enter, search out who in it is, worthy, and, there, abide, till ye go forth.

text.NRSV: As you enter the house, greet it.
text.Rotherham: And, as ye enter the house, salute it;

text.NRSV: If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
text.Rotherham: And, if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it, but, if it be not worthy, let your peace, unto you, return.

text.NRSV: If anyone will not welcome you or listen to your words, shake off the dust from your feet as you leave that house or town.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall neither welcome you, nor hear your words, as ye go forth outside that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet:

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you,—More tolerable, will it be, for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for, that, city!

text.NRSV: “See, I am sending you out like sheep into the midst of wolves; so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I, send you forth as sheep amidst wolves; become ye therefore—prudent, as serpents, and simple, as doves.

text.NRSV: Beware of them, for they will hand you over to councils and flog you in their synagogues;
text.Rotherham: But beware of men; for they will deliver you up into high-councils, and, in their synagogues, will they scourge you,—

text.NRSV: and you will be dragged before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them and the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And, before both governor and kings, shall ye be brought, for my sake—for a witness to them and the nations.

text.NRSV: When they hand you over, do not worry about how you are to speak or what you are to say; for what you are to say will be given to you at that time;
text.Rotherham: And, when they deliver you up, be not anxious how or what ye shall speak,—for it shall be given you in that hour what ye shall speak;

text.NRSV: for it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
text.Rotherham: For it is not, ye, who are speaking, but, the Spirit of your Father, that is speaking in you.

text.NRSV: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;
text.Rotherham: And, brother, will deliver up, brother, unto death, and, father, child,—and, children, will rise up, against parents, and will put them to death.

text.NRSV: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be hated by all, because of my name,—but, he that endureth throughout, the same, shall be saved.

text.NRSV: When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next; for truly I tell you, you will not have gone through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
text.Rotherham: And, when they persecute you in this city, flee into another,—for, verily, I say unto you, in nowise shall ye finish the cities of Israel, till, he Son of Man, come.

text.NRSV: “A disciple is not above the teacher, nor a slave above the master;
text.Rotherham: A, disciple, is not above, the teacher, nor, a servant, above, his lord:

text.NRSV: it is enough for the disciple to be like the teacher, and the slave like the master. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more will they malign those of his household!
text.Rotherham: Sufficient for the disciple, that he become, as his teacher, and, the servant, as, his lord. If, the master of the house, Beelzebul, they called, how much more, the men of his house!

text.NRSV: “So have no fear of them; for nothing is covered that will not be uncovered, and nothing secret that will not become known.
text.Rotherham: Then do not fear them,—for, nothing hath been covered, which shall not be, uncovered, and, hidden, which shall not be, made known.

text.NRSV: What I tell you in the dark, utter in the light; and what you hear whispered, proclaim from the housetops.
text.Rotherham: What I am saying to you in the darkness, tell ye in the light,—and, what [whispered] into the ear ye are hearing, proclaim ye on the housetops.

text.NRSV: Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
text.Rotherham: And be not in fear, by reason of them that are killing the body,—and, the soul, are not able to kill. But fear, rather, him who is able, both soul and body, to destroy in gehenna!

text.NRSV: Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from your Father.
text.Rotherham: Are not, two sparrows, for a farthing, sold? And, one from among them, shall not fall upon the ground, without your Father;

text.NRSV: And even the hairs of your head are all counted.
text.Rotherham: But, even the hairs of, your, head, have all been numbered.

text.NRSV: So do not be afraid; you are of more value than many sparrows.
text.Rotherham: Then be not in fear—than many sparrows, better are, ye!

text.NRSV: “Everyone therefore who acknowledges me before others, I also will acknowledge before my Father in heaven;
text.Rotherham: Every one therefore who shall confess me before men, I also, will confess, him, before my Father who is in the heavens;

text.NRSV: but whoever denies me before others, I also will deny before my Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: But, whoever shall deny me before men, I also, will deny, him, before my Father who is in the heavens.

text.NRSV: “Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.
