text.Rotherham: Do not think, that I came to thrust peace upon the earth,—I came not to thrust, peace, but, a sword;

text.NRSV: For I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law;
text.Rotherham: For I came to set at variance—a man, against, his father, and, a daughter, against, her mother, and, a bride, against, her mother-in-law;

text.NRSV: and one’s foes will be members of one’s own household.
text.Rotherham: And, a man foes, are, they of his own house.

text.NRSV: Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me;
text.Rotherham: He that loveth father or mother above me, is not, worthy, of me,—and, he that loveth son or daughter above me, is not, worthy, of me;

text.NRSV: and whoever does not take up the cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
text.Rotherham: And, he that doth not take his cross and follow after me, is not, worthy, of me.

text.NRSV: Those who find their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake will find it.
text.Rotherham: He that hath found his life, shall lose it,—and, he that hath lost his life, for my sake, shall find it.

text.NRSV: “Whoever welcomes you welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me.
text.Rotherham: He that giveth welcome unto you, unto me, giveth welcome, and, he that, unto me, giveth welcome, giveth welcome—unto him that sent me forth.

text.NRSV: Whoever welcomes a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward; and whoever welcomes a righteous person in the name of a righteous person will receive the reward of the righteous;
text.Rotherham: He that giveth welcome unto a prophet, in the name of a prophet, the reward of a prophet, shall receive; and, he that giveth welcome unto a righteous man, in the name of a righteous man, the reward of a righteous man, shall receive;—

text.NRSV: and whoever gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the name of a disciple—truly I tell you, none of these will lose their reward.”
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall give to drink—unto one of these little ones—a cup of cold water only, in the name of a disciple, Verily, I say unto you, in nowise, shall lose his reward!

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and proclaim his message in their cities.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished giving instructions to his twelve disciples, he passed on from thence, to be teaching and proclaiming in their cities.

text.NRSV: When John heard in prison what the Messiah was doing, he sent word by his disciples
text.Rotherham: Now, John, hearing in the prison the works of the Christ, sending through his disciples,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?”
text.Rotherham: said unto him, Art, thou, the coming one? or, a different one, are we to expect?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John what you hear and see:
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—Go report unto John what ye do hear and see:—

text.NRSV: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor have good news brought to them.
text.Rotherham: The, blind, recover sight, and, the lame, walk, lepers, are cleansed, and, the deaf, hear,—and, the dead, are raised, and, the destitute, are told the joyful tidings;

text.NRSV: And blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is he, whosoever shall not find cause of stumbling, in me.

text.NRSV: As they went away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to look at? A reed shaken by the wind?
text.Rotherham: But, as these were going their way, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes, concerning John,—What went ye forth into the wilderness to gaze at? A reed, by a wind, shaken?

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? Someone dressed in soft robes? Look, those who wear soft robes are in royal palaces.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A man, in soft clothing, arrayed? Lo! they who soft clothing do wear, are in the houses of kings.

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.Rotherham: But why went ye forth? A prophet, to see? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.

text.NRSV: This is the one about whom it is written, ‘See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he, concerning whom it is written,—Lo! I, send my messenger before thy face, who shall make ready thy way before thee!

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, among those born of women no one has arisen greater than John the Baptist; yet the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—There hath not arisen, among such as are born of women, a greater than John the Immerser,—yet, he that is less in the kingdom of the heavens, is, greater than he.

text.NRSV: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence, and the violent take it by force.
text.Rotherham: But, from the days of John the Immerser, until even now, the kingdom of the heavens, is being invaded, and, invaders, are, seizing upon it.

text.NRSV: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John came;
text.Rotherham: For, all the prophets and the law, until John, did prophesy;

text.NRSV: and if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye are willing to accept it, he, is Elijah—the one destined to come.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears listen!
text.Rotherham: He that hath ears, let him hear!

text.NRSV: “But to what will I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to one another,
text.Rotherham: But, unto what, shall I liken this generation? It is, like, unto children, sitting the market-places, who, calling unto the others,

text.NRSV: ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we wailed, and you did not mourn.’
text.Rotherham: say, We played the flute to you, and ye danced not,—we sang a lament, and ye beat not the breast.

text.NRSV: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon’;
text.Rotherham: For, John, came, neither eating nor drinking, and they say—A demon, he hath!

text.NRSV: the Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ Yet wisdom is vindicated by her deeds.”
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man, came, eating and drinking, and they say, Lo! a gluttonous man and a wine-drinker! a friend of, tax-collectors, and sinners! And yet wisdom hath been justified by her works.

text.NRSV: Then he began to reproach the cities in which most of his deeds of power had been done, because they did not repent.
text.Rotherham: Then, began he to upbraid the cities, in which had been done his noblest mighty works; because they repented not:—

text.NRSV: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
text.Rotherham: Alas for thee, Chorazin! Alas for thee, Bethsaida! Because, if, in Tyre and Zidon, had been done the works of power, which were done in you, of old, in sackcloth and ashes, had they repented.

text.NRSV: But I tell you, on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you, For Tyre and Zidon, more tolerable, will it be, in the day of judgment, than for you.

text.NRSV: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades. For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Capernaum! Unto heaven, shalt thou be uplifted? Unto hades, thou shall be brought down; because, if, in Sodom, had been done the works of power, which were done in thee, it would, in that case, have remained until this day.

text.NRSV: But I tell you that on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom than for you.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you—For the land of Sodom, more tolerable, will it be in a day of judgment, than, for thee.

text.NRSV: At that time Jesus said, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the intelligent and have revealed them to infants;
text.Rotherham: In that season, answered Jesus, and said—I openly give praise unto thee, Father, Lord of heaven and earth,—in that thou hast hid these things from the wise and discerning, and hast revealed them unto babes;

text.NRSV: yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
text.Rotherham: Yea, O Father! That, so, it hath become, a delight, before thee.

text.NRSV: All things have been handed over to me by my Father; and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
text.Rotherham: All things, unto me, have been delivered up by my Father; and, no one, fully knoweth the Son, save the rather, neither doth any one fully know, the Father, save, the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son, may be minded, to reveal, him.

text.NRSV: “Come to me, all you that are weary and are carrying heavy burdens, and I will give you rest.
text.Rotherham: Come unto me! all ye that toil and are burdened, and, I, will give you rest:

text.NRSV: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
text.Rotherham: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me,—because, meek, am I and lowly, in heart, and ye shall find rest unto your souls;

text.NRSV: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
text.Rotherham: For, my yoke, is easy, and, my burden, light.

text.NRSV: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the sabbath; his disciples were hungry, and they began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
text.Rotherham: In that season, went Jesus, on, the sabbath, through the cornfields,—and, his disciples, hungered, and began to pluck ears of corn, and to eat.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees saw it, they said to him, “Look, your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: But, the Pharisees, observing it, said unto him, Lo! thy disciples, are doing what is not allowed to do, on sabbath.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, have ye never read what, David, did, when he hungered, and they who were with him? how

text.NRSV: He entered the house of God and ate the bread of the Presence, which it was not lawful for him or his companions to eat, but only for the priests.
text.Rotherham: he entered into the house of God and, the presence-bread, did eat, which it was not, allowable, for him to eat, nor for them who were with him,—save for the priests, alone?

text.NRSV: Or have you not read in the law that on the sabbath the priests in the temple break the sabbath and yet are guiltless?
text.Rotherham: Or have ye not read, in the law, that, on the sabbaths, the priests, in the temple, the sabbath, profane, and are, blameless?

text.NRSV: I tell you, something greater than the temple is here.
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you,—Something greater than the Temple, is here!

text.NRSV: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the guiltless.
text.Rotherham: If, however, ye had known what this meaneth—Mercy, I desire, and not, sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the blameless;

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: For, the Son of Man, is, Lord of the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: He left that place and entered their synagogue;
text.Rotherham: And, passing on from thence, he came into their synagogue;

text.NRSV: a man was there with a withered hand, and they asked him, “Is it lawful to cure on the sabbath?” so that they might accuse him.
text.Rotherham: and lo! a man having, a withered hand, and they questioned him, saying, Is it allowable, on the sabbath, to heal? that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Suppose one of you has only one sheep and it falls into a pit on the sabbath; will you not lay hold of it and lift it out?
text.Rotherham: And said unto them, What man, from among yourselves, [shall there be],—Who shall have one sheep, and, if this should fall, on the sabbath, into a pit, will not lay hold of it, and raise it?

text.NRSV: How much more valuable is a human being than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: How much better, then, a man, than, a sheep? So that it is allowable, on the sabbath, nobly, to act.

text.NRSV: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and it was restored, as sound as the other.
text.Rotherham: Then saith he unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand! And he stretched it forth,—and it was restored, whole, as the other.

text.NRSV: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how to destroy him.
text.Rotherham: And the Pharisees, going forth, took, counsel, against him, to the end that, him, they might destroy.

text.NRSV: When Jesus became aware of this, he departed. Many crowds followed him, and he cured all of them,
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, taking note, retired from thence,—and many followed him, and he cured them all;

text.NRSV: and he ordered them not to make him known.
text.Rotherham: and straitly charged them, lest they should make him, manifest:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:—

text.NRSV: “Here is my servant, whom I have chosen, my beloved, with whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my servant, whom I have chosen, My beloved, in whom, my soul, delighteth,—I will put my Spirit upon him, and, justice, unto the nations, will he report:

text.NRSV: He will not wrangle or cry aloud, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
text.Rotherham: He will not strive, nor will he cry out, nor shall any hear, in the broadways, his voice:

text.NRSV: He will not break a bruised reed or quench a smoldering wick, until he brings justice to victory.
text.Rotherham: A bruised cane, will he not break, and, a smoking wick, will he not quench,—until he urge on, justice, to victory,

text.NRSV: And in his name the Gentiles will hope.”
text.Rotherham: And, in his name, shall nations hope.

text.NRSV: Then they brought to him a demoniac who was blind and mute; and he cured him, so that the one who had been mute could speak and see.
text.Rotherham: Then they brought unto him, one demonized, blind and dumb,—and he cured him, so that the dumb did speak and see.

text.NRSV: All the crowds were amazed and said, “Can this be the Son of David?”
text.Rotherham: And all the multitudes were beside themselves, and were saying, Can, this one, be, the Son of David?

text.NRSV: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “It is only by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons, that this fellow casts out the demons.”
text.Rotherham: But, the Pharisees, hearing it, said, This one, doth not cast out the demons, save in Beelzebul ruler of the demons.

text.NRSV: He knew what they were thinking and said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste, and no city or house divided against itself will stand.
text.Rotherham: And, knowing their inward thoughts, he said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself, is laid waste,—and, no city or house divided against itself, will stand;

text.NRSV: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself; how then will his kingdom stand?
text.Rotherham: And, if, Satan, is casting, Satan, out, against himself, hath he become divided,—How then shall, his kingdom, stand?

text.NRSV: If I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your own exorcists cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
text.Rotherham: And, if, I, in Beelzebul, am casting out the demons, In whom are, your sons, casting them out? Wherefore, they, shall be, judges of you.

text.NRSV: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out demons, then the kingdom of God has come to you.
text.Rotherham: But, if, in God’s Spirit, I, am casting out the demons, then doubtless hath come upon you unawares, the kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: Or how can one enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property, without first tying up the strong man? Then indeed the house can be plundered.
text.Rotherham: Or how can one enter the house of the mighty and, seize his goods, unless, first, he bind the mighty one? and, then, his house, he will plunder.

text.NRSV: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
text.Rotherham: He that is no with me, is, against me,—and, he that gathereth not with me, scattereth

text.NRSV: Therefore I tell you, people will be forgiven for every sin and blasphemy, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I say unto you, All sin and profane speaking, shall be forgiven unto men,—but, the speaking profanely of the Spirit, shall not be forgiven;

text.NRSV: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him,—but, whosoever shall speak against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, either in this age, or the coming.

text.NRSV: “Either make the tree good, and its fruit good; or make the tree bad, and its fruit bad; for the tree is known by its fruit.
text.Rotherham: Either make the tree good, and its fruit good, or make the tree worthless, and its fruit worthless; for, from the fruit, the tree is known.

text.NRSV: You brood of vipers! How can you speak good things, when you are evil? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
text.Rotherham: Broods of vipers! How can ye speak, good things, being, evil? For, out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaketh.

text.NRSV: The good person out of a good treasure brings forth good, and the evil person out of an evil treasure brings forth evil.
text.Rotherham: The good man, out of the good treasure, putteth forth, good things; and, the evil man, out of the evil treasure, putteth forth, evil things.

text.NRSV: I tell you, on the day of judgment you will have to give an account for every careless word you utter;
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you, That, every useless expression that men shall utter, they shall render, concerning it, an account, in a day of judgment;

text.NRSV: for by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
text.Rotherham: For, by thy words, shalt thou be, justified, and, by thy words, shalt thou be condemned.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, “Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you.”
text.Rotherham: Then, answered him, certain of the Scribes and Pharisees, saying, Teacher! we desire of thee, a sign, to behold.

text.NRSV: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them, A wicked and adulterous generation, a sign, doth seek, and, a sign, will not be, given, it, save the sign of Jonah the prophet.

text.NRSV: For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the sea monster, so for three days and three nights the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth.
text.Rotherham: For, just as was Jonah in the belly of the sea-monster three days and three nights, so, will be the Son of Man, in the heart of the earth, three days and three nights.

text.NRSV: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the proclamation of Jonah, and see, something greater than Jonah is here!
text.Rotherham: Men of Nineveh, will rise up in the judgment, with this generation, and will condemn it,—because they repented into the proclamation of Jonah; and lo! something greater than Jonah, here.

text.NRSV: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon, and see, something greater than Solomon is here!
text.Rotherham: The queen of the south, will arise in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it—because she came out of the ends of the earth, to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and lo! something greater than Solomon, here.

text.NRSV: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through waterless regions looking for a resting place, but it finds none.
text.Rotherham: But, when the impure spirit goeth out of the man, it passeth through waterless places, seeking rest,—and findeth it not.

text.NRSV: Then it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ When it comes, it finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
text.Rotherham: Then, it saith, Into my house, will I return whence I came out,—and, coming, findeth it empty [and] swept and adorned.

text.NRSV: Then it goes and brings along seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and live there; and the last state of that person is worse than the first. So will it be also with this evil generation.”
text.Rotherham: Then, it goeth, and taketh along with itself, seven diverse spirits, more wicked than itself,—and, entering, abideth there; and, the last state of that man, becometh, worse than the first. So, shall it be, with this, wicked, generation.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and his brothers were standing outside, wanting to speak to him.
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking unto the multitudes, lo! his mother and brethren, were standing without, seeking to speak with him.

text.NRSV: Someone told him, “Look, your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you.”
text.Rotherham: [And one said to him, Lo! thy mother and thy brethren, without, are standing, seeking, to speak, with thee.]

text.NRSV: But to the one who had told him this, Jesus replied, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto him that was telling him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?

text.NRSV: And pointing to his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
text.Rotherham: And, stretching forth his hand towards his disciples, he said, Lo! my mother and my brethren!

text.NRSV: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall do the will of my Father who is in the heavens, he, is my, brother, and sister, and mother.

text.NRSV: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat beside the sea.
text.Rotherham: On that day, Jesus, going out of the house, was sitting near the sea:

text.NRSV: Such great crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat there, while the whole crowd stood on the beach.
text.Rotherham: and there were gathered unto him, large multitudes, so that, he, into a boat, entered, and was sitting, and, all the multitude, on the beach was standing.

text.NRSV: And he told them many things in parables, saying: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto them many things, in parables, saying: Lo! the sower went forth to sow,—

text.NRSV: And as he sowed, some seeds fell on the path, and the birds came and ate them up.
text.Rotherham: and, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell by the pathway, and, the birds, came, and devoured it;

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell on rocky ground, where they did not have much soil, and they sprang up quickly, since they had no depth of soil.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell on the rocky places, where it had not much earth,—and, straightway, it sprang up, because if had no depth of earth;

text.NRSV: But when the sun rose, they were scorched; and since they had no root, they withered away.
text.Rotherham: and, the sun arising, it was scorched, and, because it had no root, it withered away;

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell upon the thorns, and the thorns came up, and choked it;

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell on good soil and brought forth grain, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
text.Rotherham: But, some, fell upon the good ground, and did yield fruit,—this, indeed a hundred fold, and, that, sixty, and, the other, thirty.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears listen!”
text.Rotherham: He that hath ears, let him hear.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples came and asked him, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, coming near, said to him, Wherefore, in parables, art thou speaking to them?

text.NRSV: He answered, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said, Because, unto you, hath it been given, to get to know the sacred secrets of the kingdom of the heavens,—whereas, unto them, hath it not been given.

text.NRSV: For to those who have, more will be given, and they will have an abundance; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever hath, it shall be given, to him, and he shall be made to abound,—but, whoever hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken from him?

text.NRSV: The reason I speak to them in parables is that ‘seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.’
text.Rotherham: For this reason, in parables, unto them, do I speak,—because, seeing, they see not, and, hearing, they hear not,—neither do they understand.

text.NRSV: With them indeed is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah that says: ‘You will indeed listen, but never understand, and you will indeed look, but never perceive.
text.Rotherham: And, again is being fulfilled in them, the prophecy of Isaiah, which saith,—They shall, surely hear, and yet will not understand, and, surely see, and yet not perceive;

text.NRSV: For this people’s heart has grown dull, and their ears are hard of hearing, and they have shut their eyes; so that they might not look with their eyes, and listen with their ears, and understand with their heart and turn—and I would heal them.’
text.Rotherham: For the heart of this people hath become dense, and, with their ears, heavily have they heard, and, their eyes, have they closed,—lest, once they should see with their eyes, and, with their ears, should hear, and, with their hearts, should understand, and return; when I would certainly heal them.

text.NRSV: But blessed are your eyes, for they see, and your ears, for they hear.
text.Rotherham: But happy are, your, eyes, that they see, and your ears, that they hear;

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see, but did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but did not hear it.
text.Rotherham: For, verily, I say unto you—Many prophets and righteous men, have coveted to see what ye see, and have not seen, and to hear what ye hear, and have not heard.

text.NRSV: “Hear then the parable of the sower.
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye, then, the parable of him that sowed:—

text.NRSV: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what is sown in the heart; this is what was sown on the path.
text.Rotherham: When anyone heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, the wicked one cometh, and catcheth up that which hath been sown in his heart,—this, is he, by the pathway, sown.

text.NRSV: As for what was sown on rocky ground, this is the one who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy;
text.Rotherham: And, he on the rocky places sown, the same, is he that, heareth the word, and, straightway, with joy, receiveth it;

text.NRSV: yet such a person has no root, but endures only for a while, and when trouble or persecution arises on account of the word, that person immediately falls away.
text.Rotherham: yet hath he no root in himself, but is, only for a season,—and, there arising, tribulation or persecution because of the word, straightway, he findeth cause of stumbling.

text.NRSV: As for what was sown among thorns, this is the one who hears the word, but the cares of the world and the lure of wealth choke the word, and it yields nothing.
text.Rotherham: And, he among the thorns sown, the same, is he that, heareth the word,—and, the anxiety of the age and the deceit of riches, choke up the word, and, unfruitful, it becometh.

text.NRSV: But as for what was sown on good soil, this is the one who hears the word and understands it, who indeed bears fruit and yields, in one case a hundredfold, in another sixty, and in another thirty.”
text.Rotherham: But, he on the good ground sown, the same, is he who doth hear and understand the word, who, indeed, beareth fruit and produceth,—this, a hundred, and, that, sixty, and, the other, thirty.

text.NRSV: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to someone who sowed good seed in his field;
text.Rotherham: Another parable, put he before them, saying—The kingdom of the heavens hath become like a man sowing good seed in his field;

text.NRSV: but while everybody was asleep, an enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and then went away.
text.Rotherham: and, while men were sleeping, his enemy came, and sowed over darnel, in among the wheat,—and away he went.

text.NRSV: So when the plants came up and bore grain, then the weeds appeared as well.
text.Rotherham: And, when the blade shot up, and brought forth, fruit, then, appeared, the darnel also.

text.NRSV: And the slaves of the householder came and said to him, ‘Master, did you not sow good seed in your field? Where, then, did these weeds come from?’
text.Rotherham: And the servants of the householder, coming near, said to him,—Sir! was it not, good seed, thou didst sow in thy field? Whence then hath it, darnel?

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘An enemy has done this.’ The slaves said to him, ‘Then do you want us to go and gather them?’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—An enemy, hath, done this. And they say, unto him—Wilt thou, then, that we go and collect it?

text.NRSV: But he replied, ‘No; for in gathering the weeds you would uproot the wheat along with them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, saith—Nay! lest, at any time, while collecting the darnel, ye uproot, along with it, the wheat:

text.NRSV: Let both of them grow together until the harvest; and at harvest time I will tell the reapers, Collect the weeds first and bind them in bundles to be burned, but gather the wheat into my barn.’”
text.Rotherham: Suffer both to grow together until the harvest, and at, harvest time, I will say unto the reapers,—Collect ye first, the darnel, and bind it into bundles, with a view to the burning it up; but, the wheat, be gathering it into my barn.

text.NRSV: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and sowed in his field;
text.Rotherham: Another parable, put he before them, saying—The kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field;

text.NRSV: it is the smallest of all the seeds, but when it has grown it is the greatest of shrubs and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and make nests in its branches.”
text.Rotherham: which, indeed, is, less, than all seeds, but, when grown, is greater than garden-plants, and becometh a tree,—so that the birds of heaven come, and lodge among its branches.

text.NRSV: He told them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed in with three measures of flour until all of it was leavened.”
text.Rotherham: Another parable, [spake he unto them]:—The kingdom of the heavens is like, unto leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour until, the whole, was leavened.

text.NRSV: Jesus told the crowds all these things in parables; without a parable he told them nothing.
text.Rotherham: All these things, spake Jesus in parables, unto the multitudes, and, without a parable, was he speaking, nothing, unto them:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet: “I will open my mouth to speak in parables; I will proclaim what has been hidden from the foundation of the world.”
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through the prophet, saying—I will open, in parables, my mouth, I will bring up things hidden from the foundation!

text.NRSV: Then he left the crowds and went into the house. And his disciples approached him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the weeds of the field.”
text.Rotherham: Then, dismissing the multitudes, he went into the house, and his disciples came near to him, saying—Make quite plain to us the parable of the darnel of the field.

text.NRSV: He answered, “The one who sows the good seed is the Son of Man;
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—He that soweth the good seed, is the Son of Man;

text.NRSV: the field is the world, and the good seed are the children of the kingdom; the weeds are the children of the evil one,
text.Rotherham: And, the field, is the world, and, the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom,—and, the darnel seeds, are the sons of the evil one;

text.NRSV: and the enemy who sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.
text.Rotherham: And, the enemy that sowed them, is the adversary, and, the harvest, is, the conclusion of an age, and, the reapers, are, messengers.

text.NRSV: Just as the weeds are collected and burned with fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
text.Rotherham: Just, therefore, as collected is the darnel, and, with fire is burned, so, will it be in the conclusion of the age:—

text.NRSV: The Son of Man will send his angels, and they will collect out of his kingdom all causes of sin and all evildoers,
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man will send forth his messengers, and they will collect, out of his kingdom, all the causes of stumbling, and the doers of lawlessness,

text.NRSV: and they will throw them into the furnace of fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: and will cast them into the furnace of fire: there, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Let anyone with ears listen!
text.Rotherham: Then, the righteous, will shine forth as the sun, in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears, let him hear!

text.NRSV: “The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field, which someone found and hid; then in his joy he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
text.Rotherham: The kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a treasure hid in the field, which a man, finding, hid,—and, by reason of his joy, withdraweth and selleth whatsoever he hath, and buyeth that field.

text.NRSV: “Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls;
text.Rotherham: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is, like, unto, a merchant, seeking beautiful pearls,—

text.NRSV: on finding one pearl of great value, he went and sold all that he had and bought it.
text.Rotherham: and, finding one very precious pearl, departing, he at once sold all things, whatsoever he had, and bought it.

text.NRSV: “Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was thrown into the sea and caught fish of every kind;
text.Rotherham: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a large drag-net, cast into the sea and gathering of every kind,—

text.NRSV: when it was full, they drew it ashore, sat down, and put the good into baskets but threw out the bad.
text.Rotherham: which, when it was filled, they dragged up on the beach, and, sitting down, collected the good into vessels, but, the worthless, forth they cast.

text.NRSV: So it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come out and separate the evil from the righteous
text.Rotherham: So, will it be in the conclusion of the age: The messengers will come forth, and separate the wicked from among the righteous;

text.NRSV: and throw them into the furnace of fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: and will cast them into the furnace of fire: there, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: “Have you understood all this?” They answered, “Yes.”
text.Rotherham: Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea!

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Therefore every scribe who has been trained for the kingdom of heaven is like the master of a household who brings out of his treasure what is new and what is old.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Wherefore, every scribe, discipled unto the kingdom of the heavens, is like unto a householder, who putteth forth out of his treasure, things new and old.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had finished these parables, he left that place.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these parables, he removed from thence;

text.NRSV: He came to his hometown and began to teach them in their synagogue, so that they were astounded and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom and these deeds of power?
text.Rotherham: and, coming into his own city, began teaching them in their synagogue, so that with astonishment were they being struck, and were saying—Whence, hath, this one, this wisdom, and the mighty works?

text.NRSV: Is not this the carpenter’s son? Is not his mother called Mary? And are not his brothers James and Joseph and Simon and Judas?
text.Rotherham: Is not, this one, the carpenter’s, son? Is not, his mother, called Mary, and are not his brethren—James and Joseph, and Simon, and Judas?

text.NRSV: And are not all his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all this?”
text.Rotherham: and, his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence, then hath, this one, all these things?

text.NRSV: And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them, “Prophets are not without honor except in their own country and in their own house.”
text.Rotherham: And they began to find cause of stumbling in him. But, Jesus, said unto them—A prophet is not without honour, save in his city, and in his house.

text.NRSV: And he did not do many deeds of power there, because of their unbelief.
text.Rotherham: And he did not, there, many mighty works, because of their unbelief.

text.NRSV: At that time Herod the ruler heard reports about Jesus;
text.Rotherham: In that season, heard Herod the tetrarch, the fame of Jesus:

text.NRSV: and he said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has been raised from the dead, and for this reason these powers are at work in him.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto his servants—This, is John the Immerser,—he hath arisen from the dead, for this cause, are the powers working mightily within him.

text.NRSV: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
text.Rotherham: For, Herod, seizing John, had bound him, and, in prison, put him away,—because of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother;

text.NRSV: because John had been telling him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
text.Rotherham: for John had been saying to him, It is not allowed thee, to have her.

text.NRSV: Though Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded him as a prophet.
text.Rotherham: And desiring, to kill, him, he feared the multitude, because, as a prophet, they held him.

text.NRSV: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before the company, and she pleased Herod
text.Rotherham: But, a birthday feast of Herod taking place, the daughter of Herodias danced in the midst, and pleased Herod;

text.NRSV: so much that he promised on oath to grant her whatever she might ask.
text.Rotherham: wherefore, with an oath, he promised to give her, whatsoever she should ask for herself;

text.NRSV: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter.”
text.Rotherham: and, she, being led on by her mother,—Give me (saith she) here, upon a charger, the head of John the Immerser.

text.NRSV: The king was grieved, yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he commanded it to be given;
text.Rotherham: And the king, though grieved, yet, because of the oaths and the guests, ordered it to be given;

text.NRSV: he sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
text.Rotherham: and sent and beheaded John in the prison.

text.NRSV: The head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, who brought it to her mother.
text.Rotherham: And his head was brought upon a charger, and given unto the maiden, and she brought it to her mother.

text.NRSV: His disciples came and took the body and buried it; then they went and told Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And his disciples, going near, bare away the corpse, and buried him, and came and brought tidings unto Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat to a deserted place by himself. But when the crowds heard it, they followed him on foot from the towns.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, hearing it, retired from thence in a boat, into a desert place, apart,—and the multitudes, hearing of it, followed him on foot from the cities.

text.NRSV: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them and cured their sick.
text.Rotherham: And, coming forth, he saw a great multitude,—and was moved with compassion over them, and cured their sick.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now late; send the crowds away so that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
text.Rotherham: And, evening, arriving, the disciples came unto him, saying—The place is, a desert, and, the hour, hath already passed,—dismiss the multitudes, that they may go away into the villages, and buy themselves food.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “They need not go away; you give them something to eat.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them, No need, have they to go away,—give, ye, them to eat.

text.NRSV: They replied, “We have nothing here but five loaves and two fish.”
text.Rotherham: But, they, say unto him—We have nothing here, save five loaves and two fishes.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Bring them here to me.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Bring, them, to me, here.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
text.Rotherham: And, giving orders that the multitudes should recline upon the grass,—taking the five loaves and the two fishes,—looking up into the heaven, he blessed; and, breaking, gave, the disciples, the loaves, and the disciples unto the multitude.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled; and they took up what was left over of the broken pieces, twelve baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they did all eat, and were filled,—and they took up the remainder of the broken pieces, twelve baskets, full.

text.NRSV: And those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
text.Rotherham: And, they who did eat, were about five thousand, men, besides women and children.

text.NRSV: Immediately he made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
text.Rotherham: And [straightway] constrained he the disciples to enter into a boat, and be going before him unto the other side, while he dismissed the multitudes.

text.NRSV: And after he had dismissed the crowds, he went up the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone,
text.Rotherham: And, dismissing the multitudes, he went up into the mountain, apart, to pray,—and when, evening, came, alone, was he, there.

text.NRSV: but by this time the boat, battered by the waves, was far from the land, for the wind was against them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the boat, still many furlongs from the land, was holding off, being distressed by the waves,—for, the wind, was, contrary.

text.NRSV: And early in the morning he came walking toward them on the sea.
text.Rotherham: And, in the fourth watch of the night, he came unto them, walking upon the sea.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified, saying, “It is a ghost!” And they cried out in fear.
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, seeing him, upon the lake walking, were troubled,—saying—It is, a ghost; and, by reason of their fear, they cried out.

text.NRSV: But immediately Jesus spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus spake unto them, saying—Take courage! it is, I,—be not afraid.

text.NRSV: Peter answered him, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Peter said unto him,—Lord! if it is, thou, bid me come unto thee, upon the waters.

text.NRSV: He said, “Come.” So Peter got out of the boat, started walking on the water, and came toward Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Come! And, descending from the boat, Peter walked upon the waters, and came unto Jesus.

text.NRSV: But when he noticed the strong wind, he became frightened, and beginning to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the wind, he was affrighted, and, beginning to sink, cried out, saying—Lord! save me!

text.NRSV: Jesus immediately reached out his hand and caught him, saying to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus, stretching forth his hand, laid hold upon him and saith unto him—O little-of-faith! why didst thou doubt?

text.NRSV: When they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came up into the boat, the wind abated.

text.NRSV: And those in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, they in the boat, bowed down to him, saying—Truly, God’s Son, thou art!

text.NRSV: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
text.Rotherham: And, going across, they came up the land, into Gennesaret.

text.NRSV: After the people of that place recognized him, they sent word throughout the region and brought all who were sick to him,
text.Rotherham: And, recognizing him, the men of that place, sent out into all that region, and they brought unto him all who were sick;

text.NRSV: and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.
text.Rotherham: and were beseeching [him], that they might, only, touch the border of his mantle, and, as many as touched, were made quite well.

text.NRSV: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
text.Rotherham: Then, there come unto Jesus from Jerusalem Pharisees and Scribes, saying—

text.NRSV: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands before they eat.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands, when they eat bread!

text.NRSV: He answered them, “And why do you break the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—Wherefore do, ye also, transgress the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?

text.NRSV: For God said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
text.Rotherham: For, God, said—Honour thy father and thy mother, and—He that revileth father or mother, let him, surely die!

text.NRSV: But you say that whoever tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is given to God,’
text.Rotherham: But, ye, say—Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother—A gift! Whatsoever, out of me, thou mightest be profited,

text.NRSV: then that person need not honor the father. So, for the sake of your tradition, you make void the word of God.
text.Rotherham: in nowise, shall honour his father or his mother—and so ye have cancelled, the word of God, for the sake of your, tradition.

text.NRSV: You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied rightly about you when he said:
text.Rotherham: Hypocrites! well prophesied concerning you, Isaiah, saying—

text.NRSV: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.Rotherham: This people, with the lips, do, honour, me, while, their heart, far off, holdeth from me;

text.NRSV: in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.’”
text.Rotherham: But, in vain, do they pay devotions unto me, teaching, for teachings, the commandments of men.

text.NRSV: Then he called the crowd to him and said to them, “Listen and understand:
text.Rotherham: And, calling near the multitude, he said to them—Hear and understand!

text.NRSV: it is not what goes into the mouth that defiles a person, but it is what comes out of the mouth that defiles.”
text.Rotherham: Not that which entereth into the mouth, defileth the man, but, that which proceedeth out of the mouth, the same, defileth the man,

text.NRSV: Then the disciples approached and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees took offense when they heard what you said?”
text.Rotherham: Then, coming near, his disciples say unto him—Knowest thou, that the Pharisees, hearing the word, were caused to stumble?

text.NRSV: He answered, “Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be uprooted.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Every plant which my heavenly Father hath not planted, will be uprooted:

text.NRSV: Let them alone; they are blind guides of the blind. And if one blind person guides another, both will fall into a pit.”
text.Rotherham: Let them alone! they are, blind leaders; and, if the, blind, lead the, blind, both, into a ditch, will fall.

text.NRSV: But Peter said to him, “Explain this parable to us.”
text.Rotherham: And Peter, answering, said unto him, Declare to us the parable.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Are you also still without understanding?
text.Rotherham: And, he, said, To this moment, are, ye also, without discernment?

text.NRSV: Do you not see that whatever goes into the mouth enters the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?
text.Rotherham: Perceive ye not that, every thing which entereth into the mouth, into the stomach, findeth way, and, into the draught, is passed;

text.NRSV: But what comes out of the mouth proceeds from the heart, and this is what defiles.
text.Rotherham: while, the things which proceed out of the mouth, out of the heart, come forth, and, they, defile the man.

text.NRSV: For out of the heart come evil intentions, murder, adultery, fornication, theft, false witness, slander.
text.Rotherham: For, out of the heart, come forth wicked designs,—murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false testimonies, profane speakings:

text.NRSV: These are what defile a person, but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile.”
text.Rotherham: these, are the things which defile the man, but, the eating with unwashed hands, doth not defile the man.

text.NRSV: Jesus left that place and withdrew to the district of Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: And, going forth from thence, Jesus retired into the parts of Tyre and Zidon.

text.NRSV: Just then a Canaanite woman from that region came out and started shouting, “Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is tormented by a demon.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! a Canaanite woman, from those bounds, coming forth, began crying out, saying, Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter, is miserably demonized.

text.NRSV: But he did not answer her at all. And his disciples came and urged him, saying, “Send her away, for she keeps shouting after us.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answered her no a word. And his disciples, coming forward began requesting him, saying—Dismiss her, because she is crying out after us.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said, I was not sent forth, save unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: But she came and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, help me.”
text.Rotherham: And, she, coming, began bowing down to him, saying, Lord! help me.

text.NRSV: He answered, “It is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said, It is, not seemly, to take the loaf of, the children, and cast, to the little dogs.

text.NRSV: She said, “Yes, Lord, yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
text.Rotherham: And, she, said, True, Lord! [for], even the little dogs, eat of the crumbs which are falling from the table of, their masters.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Let it be done for you as you wish.” And her daughter was healed instantly.
text.Rotherham: Then, answering, Jesus said to her—O woman! great, is, thy faith! Be it, done, for thee, as thou desirest. And her daughter was healed, from that hour.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had left that place, he passed along the Sea of Galilee, and he went up the mountain, where he sat down.
text.Rotherham: And, passing on from thence, Jesus came near the sea of Galilee, and, going up into the mountain, was sitting there.

text.NRSV: Great crowds came to him, bringing with them the lame, the maimed, the blind, the mute, and many others. They put them at his feet, and he cured them,
text.Rotherham: And there came unto him large multitudes, having with themselves the lame, the maimed, the blind, the dumb, and many others,—and they cast them near his feet, and he cured them;

text.NRSV: so that the crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the maimed whole, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they glorified the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: so that the multitude marvelled, seeing the dumb speaking, the lame walking, and the blind seeing,—and they glorified the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus called his disciples to him and said, “I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat; and I do not want to send them away hungry, for they might faint on the way.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, calling near his disciples, said—My compassions are moved towards the multitude, because [even now], three days, abide they with me, and they have nothing to eat,—and, to dismiss them fasting, I am not willing, lest by any means they faint in the way.

text.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Where are we to get enough bread in the desert to feed so great a crowd?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples say unto him—Whence, to us, in a wilderness, loaves in such numbers as to fill a multitude, so great?

text.NRSV: Jesus asked them, “How many loaves have you?” They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto them—How many, loaves have ye? And, they, said,—Seven, and a few small fishes.

text.NRSV: Then ordering the crowd to sit down on the ground,
text.Rotherham: And, sending word to the multitude to recline upon the ground,

text.NRSV: he took the seven loaves and the fish; and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
text.Rotherham: he took the seven loaves, and the fishes, and, giving thanks, brake,—and began giving to his disciples, and, the disciples, to the multitudes.

text.NRSV: And all of them ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they all did eat and were filled,—and, the remainder of the broken pieces, took they up, seven hampers, full.

text.NRSV: Those who had eaten were four thousand men, besides women and children.
text.Rotherham: And, they who did eat, were four thousand men, besides women and children.

text.NRSV: After sending away the crowds, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.
text.Rotherham: And, dismissing the multitudes, he went up into the boat,—and came into the bounds of Magadan.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees and Sadducees came, and to test Jesus they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees and Sadducees coming near, putting him to the test, requested him, a sign out of the heaven, to shew unto them.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said unto them—[[When evening cometh ye say, Fair! for fiery is the heaven;

text.NRSV: And in the morning, ‘It will be stormy today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but you cannot interpret the signs of the times.
text.Rotherham: And at morn, To-day, a storm! for fiery, and yet sad, is the heaven. The face of the heaven indeed, ye learn to distinguish,—but, the signs of the times, ye cannot.]]

text.NRSV: An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.” Then he left them and went away.
text.Rotherham: A wicked and adulterous generation, a sign, doth seek after, and, a sign, will not be given it,—save the sign of Jonah. And, leaving them behind, he departed.

text.NRSV: When the disciples reached the other side, they had forgotten to bring any bread.
text.Rotherham: And the disciples coming, to the other side, had forgotten to take loaves.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Watch out, and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto them—Mind! and beware, of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

text.NRSV: They said to one another, “It is because we brought no bread.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, began to deliberate among themselves, saying—Because, loaves, we took not.

text.NRSV: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said, “You of little faith, why are you talking about having no bread?
text.Rotherham: And, observing it, Jesus said—Why are ye deliberating among yourselves, ye little-of-faith! because, loaves, ye have not?

text.NRSV: Do you still not perceive? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
text.Rotherham: Not yet, perceive ye, neither remember,—The five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye received?

text.NRSV: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
text.Rotherham: Nor the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many hampers ye received?

text.NRSV: How is it that you fail to perceive that I was not speaking about bread? Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!”
text.Rotherham: How is it ye perceive not, that, not concerning loaves, spake I unto you,—but beware of the leaven, of the Pharisees and Sadducees?

text.NRSV: Then they understood that he was not telling them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
text.Rotherham: Then, understood they that he did not bid them beware of [the] leaven [of loaves] but of, the teaching, of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus came into the district of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus coming into the parts of Caesarea of Philip, began questioning his disciples, saying—Who are men saying that, the Son of Man, is?

text.NRSV: And they said, “Some say John the Baptist, but others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Some, indeed, John the Immerser, and, others, Elijah,—but, others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—But who say, ye, that I am?

text.NRSV: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text.Rotherham: And, Simon Peter, answering, said—Thou, art the Christ, the Son of the Living God.

text.NRSV: And Jesus answered him, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said to him—Happy, art thou, Simon Bar-yona,—because, flesh and blood, revealed it not unto thee, but my Father who is in the heavens.

text.NRSV: And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.
text.Rotherham: And, I also, unto thee, say—Thou, art Peter,—and, upon this rock, will I build my assembly, and, the gates of hades, shall not prevail against it.

text.NRSV: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
text.Rotherham: I will give thee, the keys of the kingdom of the heavens,—and, whatsoever thou shalt bind upon the earth, shall be bound in the heavens, and, whatsoever thou shalt loose upon the earth, shall be loosed in the heavens.

text.NRSV: Then he sternly ordered the disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: Then, straitly charged he the disciples, lest, to any man, they should say—He, is, the Christ.

text.NRSV: From that time on, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and undergo great suffering at the hands of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.
text.Rotherham: From that time, began Jesus Christ to be pointing out to his disciples that he must needs, into Jerusalem, go away, and, many things, suffer, from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be slain,—and on, the third day, arise.

text.NRSV: And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him, saying, “God forbid it, Lord! This must never happen to you.”
text.Rotherham: And, taking him aside, Peter began to rebuke him, saying—Mercy on thee Lord! In nowise, shall, this, befall thee.

text.NRSV: But he turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; for you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, turning, said to Peter—Withdraw behind me, Satan! A snare, art thou of mine, because thou art not regarding the things of God, but the things of men.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus told his disciples, “If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus said unto his disciples—If any one intendeth, after me, to come, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and be following me;

text.NRSV: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake will find it.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever intendeth, his life, to save, shall lose it,—but, whosoever shall lose his life, for my sake, shall find it.

text.NRSV: For what will it profit them if they gain the whole world but forfeit their life? Or what will they give in return for their life?
text.Rotherham: For what shall a man be profited, though, the whole world, he gain, and, his life, he forfeit? Or what shall a man give, in exchange for his life?

text.NRSV: “For the Son of Man is to come with his angels in the glory of his Father, and then he will repay everyone for what has been done.
text.Rotherham: For the Son of Man is destined to be coming, in the glory of his Father, with his messengers,—and, then, will he give back unto each one, according to his practice.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—There are some of those, here standing, who, indeed, shall in nowise taste of death, until they see the Son of Man, coming in his kingdom.

verses.text: Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and his brother John and led them up a high mountain, by themselves.
And he was transfigured before them, and his face shone like the sun, and his clothes became dazzling white.
Suddenly there appeared to them Moses and Elijah, talking with him.
Then Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here; if you wish, I will make three dwellings here, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
While he was still speaking, suddenly a bright cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, the Beloved; with him I am well pleased; listen to him!”
When the disciples heard this, they fell to the ground and were overcome by fear.
But Jesus came and touched them, saying, “Get up and do not be afraid.”
And when they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus himself alone.
As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus ordered them, “Tell no one about the vision until after the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
And the disciples asked him, “Why, then, do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
He replied, “Elijah is indeed coming and will restore all things;
but I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but they did to him whatever they pleased. So also the Son of Man is about to suffer at their hands.”
Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them about John the Baptist.
When they came to the crowd, a man came to him, knelt before him,
and said, “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is an epileptic and he suffers terribly; he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
I brought him to your disciples, but they could not cure him.”
Jesus answered, “You faithless and perverse generation, how much longer must I be with you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him here to me.”
And Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him, and the boy was cured instantly.
Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why could we not cast it out?”

He said to them, “Because of your little faith. For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.”
As they were gathering in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is to be betrayed into human hands,
and they will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised.” And they were greatly distressed.
When they reached Capernaum, the collectors of the temple tax came to Peter and said, “Does your teacher not pay the temple tax?”

He said, “Yes.” And when he came into the house, Jesus spoke to him first, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do kings of the earth take toll or tribute? From their children or from others?”
When Peter said, “From others,” Jesus said to him, “Then the children are free.

However, so that we do not give offense to them, go to the sea and cast a hook; take the first fish that comes up; and when you open its mouth, you will find a coin; take that and give it to them for you and me.”
At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?”
He called a child, whom he put among them,
and said, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
Whoever becomes humble like this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.

“If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones who believe in me, it would be better for you if a great millstone were fastened around your neck and you were drowned in the depth of the sea.
Woe to the world because of stumbling blocks! Occasions for stumbling are bound to come, but woe to the one by whom the stumbling block comes!

“If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away; it is better for you to enter life maimed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and to be thrown into the eternal fire.
And if your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out and throw it away; it is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the hell of fire.
“Take care that you do not despise one of these little ones; for I tell you, in heaven their angels continually see the face of my Father in heaven.
What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep, and one of them has gone astray, does he not leave the ninety-nine on the mountains and go in search of the one that went astray?
And if he finds it, truly I tell you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine that never went astray.
So it is not the will of your Father in heaven that one of these little ones should be lost.
“If another member of the church sins against you, go and point out the fault when the two of you are alone. If the member listens to you, you have regained that one.
But if you are not listened to, take one or two others along with you, so that every word may be confirmed by the evidence of two or three witnesses.
If the member refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church; and if the offender refuses to listen even to the church, let such a one be to you as a Gentile and a tax collector.
Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
Again, truly I tell you, if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, it will be done for you by my Father in heaven.
For where two or three are gathered in my name, I am there among them.”
Then Peter came and said to him, “Lord, if another member of the church sins against you, how often should I forgive? As many as seven times?”
Jesus said to him, “Not seven times, but, I tell you, seventy-seven times.
“For this reason the kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who wished to settle accounts with his slaves.
When he began the settlement, one who owed him ten thousand talents was brought to him;
and as he could not pay, his lord ordered him to be sold, together with his wife and children and all his possessions, and payment to be made.
So the slave fell on his knees before him, saying, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you everything.’
And out of pity for him, the lord of that slave released him and forgave him the debt.
But that same slave, as he went out, came upon one of his fellow slaves who owed him a hundred denarii; and seizing him by the throat, he said, ‘Pay what you owe.’
Then his fellow slave fell down and pleaded with him, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you.’
But he refused; then he went and threw him into prison until he would pay the debt.
When his fellow slaves saw what had happened, they were greatly distressed, and they went and reported to their lord all that had taken place.
Then his lord summoned him and said to him, ‘You wicked slave! I forgave you all that debt because you pleaded with me.
Should you not have had mercy on your fellow slave, as I had mercy on you?’
And in anger his lord handed him over to be tortured until he would pay his entire debt.
So my heavenly Father will also do to every one of you, if you do not forgive your brother or sister from your heart.”

text.NRSV: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went to the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended these words, he removed from Galilee and came into the bounds of Judaea beyond the Jordan.

text.NRSV: Large crowds followed him, and he cured them there.
text.Rotherham: And there followed him large multitudes, and he cured them there.

text.NRSV: Some Pharisees came to him, and to test him they asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any cause?”
text.Rotherham: And there came unto him Pharisees, testing him, and saying,—Whether is it allowed a man to divorce his wife, for every cause?

text.NRSV: He answered, “Have you not read that the one who made them at the beginning ‘made them male and female,’
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Did ye never read—He who created at the beginning, Male and female, made them,—

text.NRSV: and said, ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh’?
text.Rotherham: and said—For this cause, will a man leave his father and his mother, and be united to his wife,—and, the two, will become, one flesh;

text.NRSV: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
text.Rotherham: So that, the longer, are they, two, but, one flesh, What, therefore, God, hath yoked together, Let not, a man, put asunder.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Why then did Moses command us to give a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her?”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Why then did, Moses, command, to give a writing of repudiation and to divorce?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It was because you were so hard-hearted that Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—Moses, in view of your hardness of heart, permitted you to divorce your wives; From the beginning however, hath it not been done, thus.

text.NRSV: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for unchastity, and marries another commits adultery.”
text.Rotherham: And I say unto you—Whosoever shall divorce his wife, saving for unfaithfulness, and shall marry another, committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: His disciples said to him, “If such is the case of a man with his wife, it is better not to marry.”
text.Rotherham: The disciples say unto him—If, so, is the cause of the husband with the wife, it is not expedient to marry.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “Not everyone can accept this teaching, but only those to whom it is given.
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—Not all, find room for he word, save they to whom it hath been given;

text.NRSV: For there are eunuchs who have been so from birth, and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by others, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Let anyone accept this who can.”
text.Rotherham: For there, are, eunuchs, who, from their mother’s womb, were born so, and there, are, eunuchs, who were made eunuchs, by men,—and there, are, eunuchs, who have made, themselves, eunuchs, for the sake the kingdom of the heavens: He that is able to find room, let him find room.

text.NRSV: Then little children were being brought to him in order that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples spoke sternly to those who brought them;
text.Rotherham: Then, were brought unto him children,—that he might lay his hands upon them, and pray. And, the disciples, rebuked them.

text.NRSV: but Jesus said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of heaven belongs.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Suffer the children—and do not hinder them—to come unto me,—for, of such, is the kingdom of the heavens.

text.NRSV: And he laid his hands on them and went on his way.
text.Rotherham: And, laying his hands upon them, he went his way from thence.

text.NRSV: Then someone came to him and said, “Teacher, what good deed must I do to have eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And lo! one coming near unto him, said, Teacher! what good thing shall I do, that I may have life age-abiding?

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. If you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Why dost thou question me concerning that which is good? There is, One, that is good! But, if thou desirest, into life, to enter, be keeping the commandments.

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Which ones?” And Jesus said, “You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness;
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, Which? Jesus, said—These: Thou shalt not commit murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal,—Thou shalt not bear false witness;

text.NRSV: Honor your father and mother; also, You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
text.Rotherham: Honour thy father and thy mother,—and—Thou shalt love thy neighbour as, thyself.

text.NRSV: The young man said to him, “I have kept all these; what do I still lack?”
text.Rotherham: The young man saith unto him—These all, have I kept,—What, further, do I lack?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “If you wish to be perfect, go, sell your possessions, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto him—If thou desirest to be perfect, Withdraw! sell thy substance, and give to the destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in the heavens; and come! be following me.

text.NRSV: When the young man heard this word, he went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
text.Rotherham: And the young man, hearing, this word, went away sorrowing,—for he was holding large possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto his disciples—Verily, I say unto you, A rich man, with difficulty, shall enter into the kingdom of the heavens.

text.NRSV: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: Again I say unto you—Easier, is it for, a camel, through the eye of a needle, to enter, than a rich man—into the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astounded and said, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing it, the disciples were being struck with the greatest astonishment, saying—Who then can be saved?

text.NRSV: But Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but for God all things are possible.”
text.Rotherham: And, looking intently, Jesus said unto them—With men, this is, impossible, but, with God, all things are possible.

text.NRSV: Then Peter said in reply, “Look, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?”
text.Rotherham: Then, making answer, Peter said unto him—Lo! we, have left all, and followed thee,—What then shall there be, for us?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man is seated on the throne of his glory, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you, As for you who followed me in the regeneration, When the Son of Man shall take his seat on his throne of glory, ye also, shall be seated upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields, for my name’s sake, will receive a hundredfold, and will inherit eternal life.
