text.Rotherham: And, whosoever left houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or children, or lands, for sake of my name, manifold, shall receive, and life, age-abiding, shall inherit.

text.NRSV: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.
text.Rotherham: But many shall be—first-last, and last-first.

text.NRSV: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
text.Rotherham: For the kingdom of the heavens, is like, a man, a householder,—who went forth with the morning, to hire labourers into his vineyard;

text.NRSV: After agreeing with the laborers for the usual daily wage, he sent them into his vineyard.
text.Rotherham: and, when he had agreed with the labourers for a denary the day, he sent them into his vineyard.

text.NRSV: When he went out about nine o’clock, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace;
text.Rotherham: And, going forth about the third hour, he saw others, standing in the market-place, unemployed;

text.NRSV: and he said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.’ So they went.
text.Rotherham: and, to them, he said—ye also, go your way into the vineyard, and, whatsoever may be right, I will give you;

text.NRSV: When he went out again about noon and about three o’clock, he did the same.
text.Rotherham: and, they, departed. And, again, going forth about the sixth and ninth hour, he did likewise.

text.NRSV: And about five o’clock he went out and found others standing around; and he said to them, ‘Why are you standing here idle all day?’
text.Rotherham: And, about the eleventh, going forth, he found others, standing, and saith unto them—Why, here, stand ye, all the day, unemployed?

text.NRSV: They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard.’
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Because, no one, hath hired, us. He saith unto them—Ye also, go your way into the vineyard.

text.NRSV: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and give them their pay, beginning with the last and then going to the first.’
text.Rotherham: And, when evening came, the master of the vineyard saith unto his steward—Call the labourers, and pay the hire,—beginning from the last, unto the first.

text.NRSV: When those hired about five o’clock came, each of them received the usual daily wage.
text.Rotherham: And they of the eleventh hour, coming, received severally a denary.

text.NRSV: Now when the first came, they thought they would receive more; but each of them also received the usual daily wage.
text.Rotherham: And, when the first came, they supposed that, more, they should receive,—and, they also, received severally a denary.

text.NRSV: And when they received it, they grumbled against the landowner,
text.Rotherham: And, having received it, they began to murmur against the householder, saying—

text.NRSV: saying, ‘These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.’
text.Rotherham: These last, one hour, wrought, and thou hast made them, equal unto us, who have borne the burden of the day, and the scorching heat.

text.NRSV: But he replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong; did you not agree with me for the usual daily wage?
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, unto one of them, said,—Friend! I wrong thee not: Was it not, for a denary, thou didst agree with me?

text.NRSV: Take what belongs to you and go; I choose to give to this last the same as I give to you.
text.Rotherham: Take thine own, and go thy way; but I please, unto this last, to give, as also to thee:

text.NRSV: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
text.Rotherham: Is it not allowed me to do, what I please, with my own? or is, thine eye, evil, because I good?

text.NRSV: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall be—The last first, and the first last.

text.NRSV: While Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside by themselves, and said to them on the way,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, being about to go up unto Jerusalem, took unto him the twelve [disciples], apart, and, in the way, he said unto them—

text.NRSV: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death;
text.Rotherham: Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem; and, the Son of Man, will be delivered up unto the chief-priests and Scribes, and they will condemn him, [to death],

text.NRSV: then they will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified; and on the third day he will be raised.”
text.Rotherham: And deliver him up unto the nations, to mock and to scourge and to crucify,—and, on the third day, he will, arise.

text.NRSV: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, and kneeling before him, she asked a favor of him.
text.Rotherham: Then came unto him the mother of the sons of Zebedee, with her sons, bowing down, and asking something from him.

text.NRSV: And he said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Declare that these two sons of mine will sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—What desirest thou? She saith unto him—Bid, that these my two sons may sit, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left, in thy kingdom.

text.NRSV: But Jesus answered, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, answering, said—Ye know not what ye are asking: Are ye able to drink the cup, which, I, am about to drink; They say unto him—We are able.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left, this is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—My cup, indeed, ye shall drink; but, to sit on my right hand and on my left, is not mine to give,—except unto those for whom it hath been prepared by my Father.

text.NRSV: When the ten heard it, they were angry with the two brothers.
text.Rotherham: And the ten, hearing, were sorely displeased, concerning the two brethren.

text.NRSV: But Jesus called them to him and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, calling them near, said—Ye know that, the rulers of the nations, lord it over them, and, the great ones, wield authority over them:

text.NRSV: It will not be so among you; but whoever wishes to be great among you must be your servant,
text.Rotherham: Not so, is it, among you,—but, whosoever shall desire, among you, to become, great, shall be, your minister;

text.NRSV: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be your slave;
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall desire, among you, to be first, shall be, your servant:—

text.NRSV: just as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
text.Rotherham: Just as, the Son of Man, came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom instead of many.

text.NRSV: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were going forth from Jericho, there followed him a great multitude;

text.NRSV: There were two blind men sitting by the roadside. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: and lo! two blind men, sitting beside the road, hearing that Jesus was passing by, cried aloud saying—Lord! have mercy on us! Son of David!

text.NRSV: The crowd sternly ordered them to be quiet; but they shouted even louder, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: But the multitude rebuked them, that they might hold their peace. But, they, the more, cried aloud, saying—Lord, have mercy on us! Son of David!

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and called them, saying, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text.Rotherham: And, standing still, Jesus called them, and said—What desire ye I should do for you?

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Lord! that, our eyes, may open.

text.NRSV: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they regained their sight and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes,—and, straightway, they recovered sight, and followed him.

text.NRSV: When they had come near Jerusalem and had reached Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
text.Rotherham: And, when they drew near unto Jerusalem, and came unto Bethphage, unto the Mount of Olives, then, Jesus sent forth two disciples;

text.NRSV: saying to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to me.
text.Rotherham: saying unto them—Be going into the village that is over against you, and, straightway, ye shall find an ass, bound, and a colt with her,—loose them, and lead them unto me.

text.NRSV: If anyone says anything to you, just say this, ‘The Lord needs them.’ And he will send them immediately.”
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone, unto you, say aught, ye shall say—Their Lord, hath, need,—and, straightway, he will send them.

text.NRSV: This took place to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet, saying,
text.Rotherham: But, this, hath come to pass, that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through the prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “Tell the daughter of Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
text.Rotherham: Tell ye the daughter of Zion, Lo! thy King, is coming unto thee, meek and mounted upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of a toiling ass.

text.NRSV: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them;
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, went, and did as Jesus directed them;

text.NRSV: they brought the donkey and the colt, and put their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.
text.Rotherham: and led the ass and the colt, and laid upon them their garments, and he took his seat upon them.

text.NRSV: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
text.Rotherham: And, the chief multitude, spread their own mantles in the way, and, others, were cutting off young branches from the trees, and spreading them in the way;

text.NRSV: The crowds that went ahead of him and that followed were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
text.Rotherham: and the multitudes, they who were going before him and they who were following after, were crying aloud, saying—Hosanna! to the Son of David, Blessed is he that is coming in the name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest.

text.NRSV: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in turmoil, asking, “Who is this?”
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered into Jerusalem, all the city was startled, saying—Who is this?

text.NRSV: The crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, were saying—This, is the prophet Jesus,—He from Nazareth of Galilee.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus entered the temple and drove out all who were selling and buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus entered into the temple, and cast out all who were selling and buying in the temple,—and, the tables of the money-changers, he overthrew, and the seats of them who were selling doves;

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer’; but you are making it a den of robbers.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—It is written, My house, a house of prayer, shall be called; but, ye, are making it, A den of robbers!

text.NRSV: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he cured them.
text.Rotherham: And there came unto him blind and lame, in the temple, and he cured them.

text.NRSV: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the amazing things that he did, and heard the children crying out in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they became angry
text.Rotherham: But the Chief-priests and the Scribes, seeing, the marvels that he wrought, and the boys who were crying aloud in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David! were greatly displeased;

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes; have you never read, ‘Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise for yourself’?”
text.Rotherham: and said unto him—Hearest thou what these are saying? And, Jesus, saith unto them—Yea: Have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings, hast thou prepared praise?

text.NRSV: He left them, went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
text.Rotherham: And, leaving them behind, he went forth outside the city into Bethany, and spent the night there.

text.NRSV: In the morning, when he was returning to the city, he was hungry.
text.Rotherham: And, early, returning into the city, he hungered;

text.NRSV: And seeing a fig tree by the side of the road, he went to it and found nothing at all on it but leaves. Then he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” And the fig tree withered at once.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing one fig-tree by the way, he came up to it, and nothing, found he thereon, save leaves only,—and he saith unto it—No more, from thee, let fruit spring forth, unto times age-abiding,—and the fig-tree, instantly withered away.

text.NRSV: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed, saying, “How did the fig tree wither at once?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, seeing it, marvelled, saying—How instantly, did the fig-tree wither away.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you, If ye have faith, and do not doubt, not only, this of the fig-tree, shall ye do,—but, even if, unto this mountain, ye shall say, Be lifted up, and be cast into the sea, it shall be done.

text.NRSV: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.”
text.Rotherham: And, all things whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.

text.NRSV: When he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
text.Rotherham: And, when he, entered, the temple, the Chief-priests and the Elders of the people, came unto him as he was teaching, saying, By what authority, these things, art thou doing? and, who, to thee, gave, this authority?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I will also ask you one question; if you tell me the answer, then I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Jesus said unto them—I also, will ask, you, one thing,—which if ye tell me, I also, will tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing:—

text.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?” And they argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
text.Rotherham: The immersion by John, whence was it? Of heaven, or of men? But, they, began to deliberate among themselves, saying—If we say, Of heaven; He will say unto us, Wherefore then did ye not believe him?

text.NRSV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we are afraid of the crowd; for all regard John as a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: But, if we say, Of men, we fear the multitude, for, all, as a prophet, are holding John.

text.NRSV: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
text.Rotherham: And, making answer to Jesus, they said—We know not. He also, said unto them—Neither do, I, tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing.

text.NRSV: “What do you think? A man had two sons; he went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
text.Rotherham: But how, to you, doth it seem? A man, had two sons: coming unto the first, he said, Son! go thy way, to-day, be working in the vineyard.

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘I will not’; but later he changed his mind and went.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said, I, sir! and went not;

text.NRSV: The father went to the second and said the same; and he answered, ‘I go, sir’; but he did not go.
text.Rotherham: And, coming unto the second, he spake, in like manner,—and, he, answering, said, I will not: afterwards, smitten with regret, he went.

text.NRSV: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are going into the kingdom of God ahead of you.
text.Rotherham: Which of the two, did the will of the father? They say, The latter. Jesus saith unto them—Verily, I say unto you, the tax-collectors and the harlots, are going before you, into the kingdom of God;

text.NRSV: For John came to you in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him; and even after you saw it, you did not change your minds and believe him.
text.Rotherham: For John came unto you, in a way of righteousness, and ye believed him not,—but, the tax-collectors and the harlots, believed him; and, ye, seeing it, were not even smitten with regret, afterwards, so as to believe him.

text.NRSV: “Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a wine press in it, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
text.Rotherham: Another parable, hear ye:—A man there was, a householder, who planted a vineyard, and, a wall around it, placed, and digged in it a wine-vat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen,—and left home.

text.NRSV: When the harvest time had come, he sent his slaves to the tenants to collect his produce.
text.Rotherham: And, when the season of fruits drew near, He sent forth his servants unto the husbandmen to receive his fruits.

text.NRSV: But the tenants seized his slaves and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
text.Rotherham: And the husbandmen, taking his servants, one, indeed, they beat, and, another, slew,—and, another, stoned.

text.NRSV: Again he sent other slaves, more than the first; and they treated them in the same way.
text.Rotherham: Again, sent he forth other servants, more than the first, and they did unto them, likewise.

text.NRSV: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, however, he sent forth unto them his son, saying,—They will pay deference unto my son!

text.NRSV: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him and get his inheritance.’
text.Rotherham: But, the husbandmen, seeing the son, said among themselves,—This, is, the heir: Come on! Let us slay him, and have his inheritance.

text.NRSV: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text.Rotherham: And, taking him, they cast him forth outside the vineyard,—and slew him.

text.NRSV: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
text.Rotherham: When therefore the master of the vineyard shall come, what will he do unto those husbandmen?

text.NRSV: They said to him, “He will put those wretches to a miserable death, and lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the produce at the harvest time.”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Miserable men! Miserably, will he destroy them; and will let out, the vineyard, to other husbandmen, who will render unto him the fruits in their seasons.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the scriptures: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Have ye, never, read in the Scriptures, A stone which the builders rejected, the same, hath become, head of the corner: From the Lord, hath this come to pass, and is marvellous in our eyes.

text.NRSV: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people that produces the fruits of the kingdom.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore I say unto you, The kingdom of God, will be taken away from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof:

text.NRSV: The one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
text.Rotherham: [And, he that falleth on this stone, will be sorely bruised; but, on whomsoever it may fall, it will utterly destroy him.]

text.NRSV: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized that he was speaking about them.
text.Rotherham: And the Chief-priests and the Pharisees, hearing his parables, took note, that, concerning them, he was speaking.

text.NRSV: They wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, because they regarded him as a prophet.
text.Rotherham: And, seeking to secure him, they feared the multitudes; since, for a prophet, were they holding him.

text.NRSV: Once more Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying:
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus, again, spake in parables unto them, saying:—

text.NRSV: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text.Rotherham: The kingdom of the heavens hath become like a man, a king,—who made a marriage-feast for his son;

text.NRSV: He sent his slaves to call those who had been invited to the wedding banquet, but they would not come.
text.Rotherham: And sent his servants, to call the invited into the marriage-feast,—and they would not come.

text.NRSV: Again he sent other slaves, saying, ‘Tell those who have been invited: Look, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready; come to the wedding banquet.’
text.Rotherham: Again, sent he other servants, saying—Say to the invited, Lo! my dinner, have I prepared, mine oxen and my fatlings, are slain,—and, all things, are, ready: Come ye into the marriage-feast.

text.NRSV: But they made light of it and went off, one to his farm, another to his business,
text.Rotherham: And, they, slighting it, went off, one, indeed, into his own field, and, another, unto his merchandise,—

text.NRSV: while the rest seized his slaves, mistreated them, and killed them.
text.Rotherham: And, the rest, securing his servants, ill-treated and slew them.

text.NRSV: The king was enraged. He sent his troops, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
text.Rotherham: And, the king, was provoked to anger,—and, sending his armies, destroyed those murderers, and, their city, set on fire.

text.NRSV: Then he said to his slaves, ‘The wedding is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto his servants—The marriage, indeed, is ready,—but, the invited, were not, worthy;

text.NRSV: Go therefore into the main streets, and invite everyone you find to the wedding banquet.’
text.Rotherham: Be going, therefore, into the crossways of the roads, and, as many as ye shall find, call ye into the marriage-feast.

text.NRSV: Those slaves went out into the streets and gathered all whom they found, both good and bad; so the wedding hall was filled with guests.
text.Rotherham: And those servants, going forth, into the roads, gathered together all whom they found, both bad and good,—and filled was the bride-chamber with guests.

text.NRSV: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing a wedding robe,
text.Rotherham: But the king, entering to view the guests, saw there a man, who had not put on a wedding-garment,—

text.NRSV: and he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding robe?’ And he was speechless.
text.Rotherham: And saith unto him—Friend! how camest thou in here, not having a wedding-garment? And, he, was put to silence.

text.NRSV: Then the king said to the attendants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
text.Rotherham: Then, the king said unto the ministers, Binding him feet and hands, cast him forth into the darkness, outside: There, shall be wafting and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: For many are called, but few are chosen.”
text.Rotherham: For, many, are called, but, few, chosen.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees went and plotted to entrap him in what he said.
text.Rotherham: Then, went the Pharisees and took, counsel, that they might ensnare him, in discourse.

text.NRSV: So they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and teach the way of God in accordance with truth, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality.
text.Rotherham: And they sent forth to him their disciples, with the Herodians, saying, Teacher! we know that, true, thou art, and, the way of God, in truth, dost teach, and it concerneth thee not about anyone,—for thou lookest not unto the face of men:

text.NRSV: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?”
text.Rotherham: Tell us then, how, to thee, it seemeth? Is it allowable to give tax unto Caesar, or not?

text.NRSV: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, “Why are you putting me to the test, you hypocrites?
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, taking note of their wickedness, said—Why are ye tempting me, hypocrites?

text.NRSV: Show me the coin used for the tax.” And they brought him a denarius.
text.Rotherham: Shew me the coin appointed for the tax. And, they, brought unto him a denary.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Whose, is this image and the inscription?

text.NRSV: They answered, “The emperor’s.” Then he said to them, “Give therefore to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
text.Rotherham: They say—Caesar’s. Then, saith he unto them—Render, therefore, the things of Caesar, unto Caesar,—and, the things of God, unto God.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were amazed; and they left him and went away.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing, they marvelled,—and, leaving him, departed.

text.NRSV: The same day some Sadducees came to him, saying there is no resurrection; and they asked him a question, saying,
text.Rotherham: On that day, there came unto him Sadducees, who say, there is, no resurrection—and they questioned him,

text.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies childless, his brother shall marry the widow, and raise up children for his brother.’
text.Rotherham: saying—Teacher! Moses, said, If any man die not having children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

text.NRSV: Now there were seven brothers among us; the first married, and died childless, leaving the widow to his brother.
text.Rotherham: Now there were, with us, seven brethren; and, the first, marrying, died, and not having seed, left his wife unto his brother.

text.NRSV: The second did the same, so also the third, down to the seventh.
text.Rotherham: Likewise, the second also, and the third,—unto the seven.

text.NRSV: Last of all, the woman herself died.
text.Rotherham: And, last of all, died, the wife.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection, then, whose wife of the seven will she be? For all of them had married her.”
text.Rotherham: In the resurrection, therefore,—Of which of the seven, shall she be wife? For, all, had her.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “You are wrong, because you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus answering, said—Ye are deceiving yourselves, knowing neither the Scriptures, nor yet the power of God.

text.NRSV: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
text.Rotherham: For, in the resurrection, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but, as messengers in the heaven, are they.

text.NRSV: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God,
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the resurrection of the dead—Have ye not read what was spoken unto you by God, saying—

text.NRSV: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is God not of the dead, but of the living.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?—He is not God, of the dead, but, of the living.

text.NRSV: And when the crowd heard it, they were astounded at his teaching.
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, hearing, were being struck with astonishment at his teaching.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together,
text.Rotherham: Now, the Pharisees, hearing that he had silenced the Sadducees, were brought together with one accord;

text.NRSV: and one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.
text.Rotherham: and one from among them, a lawyer, proposed a question, putting him to the test:

text.NRSV: “Teacher, which commandment in the law is the greatest?”
text.Rotherham: Teacher! which commandment, is greatest in the law?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Thou shalt love the Lord thy God—with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind:

text.NRSV: This is the greatest and first commandment.
text.Rotherham: This, is the great and first commandment.

text.NRSV: And a second is like it: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
text.Rotherham: The second, like it, is, this: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

text.NRSV: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: In these two commandments, all the law, is contained, and the prophets.

text.NRSV: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them this question:
text.Rotherham: Now, the Pharisees having come together, Jesus questioned them, saying—

text.NRSV: “What do you think of the Messiah? Whose son is he?” They said to him, “The son of David.”
text.Rotherham: How, to you, doth it seem, concerning the Christ? Whose son, is he? They say unto him—David’s.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “How is it then that David by the Spirit calls him Lord, saying,
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—How then doth, David, in spirit, call him, Lord, saying—

text.NRSV: ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet”’?
text.Rotherham: The Lord, hath said unto, my Lord,—Sit thou on my right hand, until I make thy foes thy footstool?

text.NRSV: If David thus calls him Lord, how can he be his son?”
text.Rotherham: If then, David, calleth him, Lord, How, is he, his son?

text.NRSV: No one was able to give him an answer, nor from that day did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, was able to answer him a word, neither durst anyone, from that day, question him, any more.

verses.text: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples,
“The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses’ seat;
therefore, do whatever they teach you and follow it; but do not do as they do, for they do not practice what they teach.
They tie up heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on the shoulders of others; but they themselves are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
They do all their deeds to be seen by others; for they make their phylacteries broad and their fringes long.
They love to have the place of honor at banquets and the best seats in the synagogues,
and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have people call them rabbi.
But you are not to be called rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all students.
And call no one your father on earth, for you have one Father—the one in heaven.
Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Messiah.
The greatest among you will be your servant.
All who exalt themselves will be humbled, and all who humble themselves will be exalted.
“But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. For you do not go in yourselves, and when others are going in, you stop them.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cross sea and land to make a single convert, and you make the new convert twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.
“Woe to you, blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever swears by the sanctuary, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the sanctuary is bound by the oath.’
You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the sanctuary that has made the gold holy?
And you say, ‘Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on the altar is bound by the oath.’
How blind you are! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?
So whoever swears by the altar, swears by it and by everything on it;
and whoever swears by the sanctuary, swears by it and by the one who dwells in it;
and whoever swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by the one who is seated upon it.

“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faith. It is these you ought to have practiced without neglecting the others.
You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel!
“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the plate, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup, so that the outside also may become clean.
“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which on the outside look beautiful, but inside they are full of the bones of the dead and of all kinds of filth.
So you also on the outside look righteous to others, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous,
and you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’
Thus you testify against yourselves that you are descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
Fill up, then, the measure of your ancestors.
You snakes, you brood of vipers! How can you escape being sentenced to hell?
Therefore I send you prophets, sages, and scribes, some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town;
so that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
Truly I tell you, all this will come upon this generation.

“Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to it! How often have I desired to gather your children together as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you were not willing!
See, your house is left to you, desolate.
For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, ‘Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.’”

text.NRSV: As Jesus was leaving the temple and was walking away, his disciples came to him to point out the buildings of the temple.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus coming forth, from the temple, was taking his departure, when his disciples came forward to point out to him the buildings of the temple.

text.NRSV: Then he asked them, “You see all these, do you not? Truly I tell you, not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—Are ye not beholding all these things? Verily, I say unto you—in no wise, shall there be left here, stone upon stone, which shall not be thrown down.

text.NRSV: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was sitting upon the Mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him, privately, saying—Tell us, when these things shall be,—and what the sign of thy presence, and the conclusion of the age.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Beware that no one leads you astray.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—Be taking heed, lest anyone, deceive, you;

text.NRSV: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Messiah!’ and they will lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: For, many, will come upon my name, saying—I, am the Christ,—and will, deceive many.

text.NRSV: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that you are not alarmed; for this must take place, but the end is not yet.
text.Rotherham: Moreover ye will be sure to be hearing of wars, and rumours of wars: Mind! be not alarmed, for it must needs happen,—but, not yet, is, the end;

text.NRSV: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places:
text.Rotherham: For there will arise—nation against nation, and kingdom against kingdom,—and there will be famines and earthquakes, in places.

text.NRSV: all this is but the beginning of the birthpangs.
text.Rotherham: But, all these things, are a beginning of birth-pangs.

text.NRSV: “Then they will hand you over to be tortured and will put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of my name.
text.Rotherham: Then, will they deliver you up into tribulation, and will slay you,—and ye will be men hated by all the nations, because of my name

text.NRSV: Then many will fall away, and they will betray one another and hate one another.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will many be caused to stumble, and, one another, will deliver up, and will hate one another;

text.NRSV: And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: And, many false prophets, will arise, and deceive, many;

text.NRSV: And because lawlessness is increased, the love of many will grow cold.
text.Rotherham: And, because of lawlessness being brought to the full, the love of the many, will grew cold.

text.NRSV: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath endured throughout, the same, shall be saved.

text.NRSV: And this good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world, as a testimony to all the nations; and then the end will come.
text.Rotherham: And this glad message of the kingdom will be proclaimed in all the inhabited earth, for a witness unto all the nations,—and, then, will have come, the end.

text.NRSV: “So when you see the desolating sacrilege standing in the holy place, as was spoken of by the prophet Daniel (let the reader understand),
text.Rotherham: Whensoever, therefore, ye shall see the abomination desolation, that was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in a holy place,—he that readeth, let him think,

text.NRSV: then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;
text.Rotherham: Then, they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains;

text.NRSV: the one on the housetop must not go down to take what is in the house;
text.Rotherham: And, he that is on the house-top, let him not come down, to take away the things out of his house;

text.NRSV: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.
text.Rotherham: And, he that is in the field, let him not turn back, to take away his mantle.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!
text.Rotherham: But alas! for the women with child, and for them that are giving suck, in those days;

text.NRSV: Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a sabbath.
text.Rotherham: But be praying that your flight, may not happen in winter, nor on sabbath;

text.NRSV: For at that time there will be great suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, and never will be.
text.Rotherham: For there will be then, Great tribulation, such as hath not happened, from the beginning of the world, until the present time, neither in any wise shall happen.

text.NRSV: And if those days had not been cut short, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
text.Rotherham: And, except those days had been shortened, no flesh had been saved; but, for the sake of the chosen, those days shall be shortened.

text.NRSV: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look! Here is the Messiah!’ or ‘There he is!’—do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: Then, if any, unto you, say—Lo! here, is the Christ, or, there, Do not believe it;

text.NRSV: For false messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce great signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise, false Christs, and false prophets,—and they will show great signs and wonders, so that, if possible, even the chosen, should be deceived:—

text.NRSV: Take note, I have told you beforehand.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have foretold you.

text.NRSV: So, if they say to you, ‘Look! He is in the wilderness,’ do not go out. If they say, ‘Look! He is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: If therefore they should say unto you, Lo! in the desert, he is, Do not go forth; Lo! in the chambers, Do not believe it.

text.NRSV: For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the lightning goeth forth from the east, and shineth unto the west, so, shall be the presence of the Son of Man.

text.NRSV: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever, the corpse, shall be, there, shall be gathered, the vultures!

text.NRSV: “Immediately after the suffering of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of heaven will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: But, straightway after the tribulation of those days, the sun, will be darkened, and, the moon, will not give her brightness, and, the stars, will fall from heaven,—and, the powers of the heavens, will be shaken;

text.NRSV: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will be displayed—The sign of the Son of Man in heaven, and, then, will smite their breasts—all the tribes of the earth; and they will see the Son of Man—coming upon the clouds of heaven, with great power and glory.

text.NRSV: And he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
text.Rotherham: And he will send forth his messengers, with a great trumpet, and they will gather together his chosen—Out of the four winds, from heavens’ bounds, unto their bounds.

text.NRSV: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.Rotherham: Now, from the fig-tree, learn ye, the parable:—When, already, her young branch, becometh tender, and the leaves, may be sprouting, ye observe, that, near, is, the summer:

text.NRSV: So also, when you see all these things, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, when ye shall see all these things, observe ye, that, near, he is, at the doors.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise, shall this generation, pass away, until all these things, shall happen:

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: The heaven and the earth, shall pass away, but, my words, shall, in nowise, pass away.

text.NRSV: “But about that day and hour no one knows, neither the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning that day and hour, no one, knoweth, neither the messengers of the heavens, nor the Son,—save the Father only.

text.NRSV: For as the days of Noah were, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the days of Noah, so, will be the presence of the Son of Man;

text.NRSV: For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark,
text.Rotherham: For, as they were in those days that were before the flood, feeding and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage,—until the day Noah entered into the ark;

text.NRSV: and they knew nothing until the flood came and swept them all away, so too will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: And they observed not, until the flood came and took away all together, so, will be, the presence of the Son of Man.

text.NRSV: Then two will be in the field; one will be taken and one will be left.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall there be two men in the field,—one, is taken near, and, one, is left behind:

text.NRSV: Two women will be grinding meal together; one will be taken and one will be left.
text.Rotherham: Two women grinding at the mill,—one, is taken near, and, one, is left behind.

text.NRSV: Keep awake therefore, for you do not know on what day your Lord is coming.
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, for ye know not, on what manner of day, your Lord is coming.

text.NRSV: But understand this: if the owner of the house had known in what part of the night the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and would not have let his house be broken into.
text.Rotherham: But there is, one thing, ye know—That, if the householder, had known, in what watch, the thief was coming, he would have been on the alert, and not have suffered his house to be dug through.

text.NRSV: Therefore you also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an unexpected hour.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, ye also, be getting ready, because, in what hour ye are, not thinking, The Son of Man doth come.

text.NRSV: “Who then is the faithful and wise slave, whom his master has put in charge of his household, to give the other slaves their food at the proper time?
text.Rotherham: Who then is the faithful and prudent servant, whom the master hath appointed over his household, to give them food in season?

text.NRSV: Blessed is that slave whom his master will find at work when he arrives.
text.Rotherham: Happy! that servant, whom his master, when he cometh, shall find so doing!

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, he will put that one in charge of all his possessions.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Over all his substance, will he appoint him.

text.NRSV: But if that wicked slave says to himself, ‘My master is delayed,’
text.Rotherham: But, if that wicked servant should say in his heart—My master, delayeth,

text.NRSV: and begins to beat his fellow slaves, and eats and drinks with drunkards,
text.Rotherham: And begin to be striking his fellow-servants, and eating and drinking with the drunken,

text.NRSV: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour that he does not know.
text.Rotherham: The master of that servant, will have come, on a day when he is not expecting, and in an hour when he is not observing,—

text.NRSV: He will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: And will cut him asunder; and, his part, with the hypocrites, will appoint: There, shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth!

text.NRSV: “Then the kingdom of heaven will be like this. Ten bridesmaids took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
text.Rotherham: Then, will the kingdom of the heavens become like unto, ten virgins, who, taking their torches, went forth to meet the bridegroom.

text.NRSV: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
text.Rotherham: Now, five of them, were foolish, and, five, prudent.

text.NRSV: When the foolish took their lamps, they took no oil with them;
text.Rotherham: For, the foolish—though they took, their torches, took not with them, oil:

text.NRSV: but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.
text.Rotherham: But, the prudent, took oil in their vessels, with their torches.

text.NRSV: As the bridegroom was delayed, all of them became drowsy and slept.
text.Rotherham: Now, the bridegroom, delaying, they all became drowsy, and were sleeping.

text.NRSV: But at midnight there was a shout, ‘Look! Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.’
text.Rotherham: And, at midnight, an outcry hath been made—Lo! the bridegroom! Be going forth to meet him!

text.NRSV: Then all those bridesmaids got up and trimmed their lamps.
text.Rotherham: Then, arose all those virgins, and trimmed their torches.

text.NRSV: The foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
text.Rotherham: And, the foolish, unto the prudent, said—Give us of your oil, because, our torches, are going out.

text.NRSV: But the wise replied, ‘No! there will not be enough for you and for us; you had better go to the dealers and buy some for yourselves.’
text.Rotherham: But the prudent, answered, saying—Lest once, by any means, there be not enough for us and you, be going rather unto them that sell, and buy for yourselves

text.NRSV: And while they went to buy it, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went with him into the wedding banquet; and the door was shut.
text.Rotherham: But, as they were going away to buy, the bridegroom came; and, they who were ready, went in with him, into the marriage-feast, and, the door was locked.

text.NRSV: Later the other bridesmaids came also, saying, ‘Lord, lord, open to us.’
text.Rotherham: But, afterwards, came the other virgins also, saying—Lord! Lord! open unto us!

text.NRSV: But he replied, ‘Truly I tell you, I do not know you.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Verily, I say unto you, I know you not.

text.NRSV: Keep awake therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour.
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, because ye know, neither the day nor the hour.

text.NRSV: “For it is as if a man, going on a journey, summoned his slaves and entrusted his property to them;
text.Rotherham: For it is, just as a man, who, going from home, called his own servants, and delivered up to them his substance;

text.NRSV: to one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away.
text.Rotherham: And, to one, indeed, gave he five talents, and, to another, two, and, to another, one,—to each according to his particular ability, and went from home.

text.NRSV: The one who had received the five talents went off at once and traded with them, and made five more talents.
text.Rotherham: Straightway, he who, the five talents, had received, went and traded with them, and gained other five:

text.NRSV: In the same way, the one who had the two talents made two more talents.
text.Rotherham: Likewise, he of the two, gained other two:

text.NRSV: But the one who had received the one talent went off and dug a hole in the ground and hid his master’s money.
text.Rotherham: But, he who the one had received, went away, and digged up ground, and hid the silver of his lord.

text.NRSV: After a long time the master of those slaves came and settled accounts with them.
text.Rotherham: And, after a long time, cometh the lord of those servants, and reckoneth with them.

text.NRSV: Then the one who had received the five talents came forward, bringing five more talents, saying, ‘Master, you entrusted to me five talents; see, I have made five more talents.’
text.Rotherham: And he who the five talents had received, coming forward, brought other five talents, saying,—Lord! five talents, to me, thou didst deliver up: See! other five talents, I gained.

text.NRSV: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and trustworthy slave; you have been trustworthy in a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.’
text.Rotherham: His lord said unto him—Well-done! good and faithful servant,—Over a few things wast thou faithful, over many things, will I appoint thee: Enter into the joy of thy lord.

text.NRSV: And the one with the two talents also came forward, saying, ‘Master, you entrusted to me two talents; see, I have made two more talents.’
text.Rotherham: He also of the two talents, coming forward, said—Lord! two talents, to me, didst thou deliver up: See! other two talents, I gained.

text.NRSV: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and trustworthy slave; you have been trustworthy in a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.’
text.Rotherham: His lord said unto him—Well-done! good and faithful servant,—Over a few things, wast thou faithful, over many things, will I appoint thee: Enter into the joy of thy lord.

text.NRSV: Then the one who had received the one talent also came forward, saying, ‘Master, I knew that you were a harsh man, reaping where you did not sow, and gathering where you did not scatter seed;
text.Rotherham: But he also who, the one talent, had received, coming forward, said—Lord I knew thee, that thou art a, hard, man, reaping, where thou hast not sown,—and gathering, whence thou hast not winnowed;

text.NRSV: so I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground. Here you have what is yours.’
text.Rotherham: And, overcome with fear, I went away, and hid thy talent in the ground: See! thou hast what is thine!

text.NRSV: But his master replied, ‘You wicked and lazy slave! You knew, did you, that I reap where I did not sow, and gather where I did not scatter?
text.Rotherham: And his lord, answering, said unto him—O wicked servant, and cowardly, knewest thou that I reap, where I have not sown, and gather, whence I have not winnowed?

text.NRSV: Then you ought to have invested my money with the bankers, and on my return I would have received what was my own with interest.
text.Rotherham: It was binding on thee, therefore, to cast my silver into the money-changers,—and, I, when I, came, might have obtained what was mine, with interest.

text.NRSV: So take the talent from him, and give it to the one with the ten talents.
text.Rotherham: Therefore take away, from him, the talent, and give unto him that hath the ten talents;

text.NRSV: For to all those who have, more will be given, and they will have an abundance; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: For, to every one that hath, shall be given, and he shall be made to abound; but, from him that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken away from him:

text.NRSV: As for this worthless slave, throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
text.Rotherham: And, the unprofitable servant, cast ye forth into the darkness, outside: There, shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever the Son of Man shall come in his glory, and all the messengers with him, then will he sit on his throne of glory;

text.NRSV: All the nations will be gathered before him, and he will separate people one from another as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats,
text.Rotherham: And there will be gathered before him, all the nations, and he will separate them one from another, just as, the shepherd! separateth the sheep from the goats,—

text.NRSV: and he will put the sheep at his right hand and the goats at the left.
text.Rotherham: And he will set, the sheep, indeed, on his right hand, but, the goats, on the left.

text.NRSV: Then the king will say to those at his right hand, ‘Come, you that are blessed by my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;
text.Rotherham: Then, will the king say to those on his right hand: Come ye, the blessed of my Father! Inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;

text.NRSV: for I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me something to drink, I was a foreigner and you welcomed me,
text.Rotherham: For I hungered, and ye gave me to eat, I thirsted, and ye gave me drink, a stranger, was I, and ye took me home,

text.NRSV: I was naked and you gave me clothing, I was sick and you took care of me, I was in prison and you visited me.’
text.Rotherham: Naked, and ye clothed me, sick, and ye visited me, in prison, was I, and ye came unto me.

text.NRSV: Then the righteous will answer him, ‘Lord, when was it that we saw you hungry and gave you food, or thirsty and gave you something to drink?
text.Rotherham: Then, will the righteous answer him, saying: Lord! when, saw we thee—hungry, and fed thee, or thirsty, and gave thee drink?

text.NRSV: And when was it that we saw you a foreigner and welcomed you, or naked and gave you clothing?
text.Rotherham: And, when, saw we thee—a stranger, and took thee home, or naked, and clothed thee?

text.NRSV: And when was it that we saw you sick or in prison and visited you?’
text.Rotherham: And, when, saw we thee—sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?

text.NRSV: And the king will answer them, ‘Truly I tell you, just as you did it to one of the least of these who are members of my family, you did it to me.’
text.Rotherham: And, answering, the king will say unto them: Verily, I say unto you—Inasmuch as ye did it unto one of these my least brethren, unto me, ye did it.

text.NRSV: Then he will say to those at his left hand, ‘You that are accursed, depart from me into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels;
text.Rotherham: Then, will he say unto those also, on his left hand: Depart ye from me, accursed ones! Into the age-abiding fire, which hath been prepared for the adversary and his messengers;

text.NRSV: for I was hungry and you gave me no food, I was thirsty and you gave me nothing to drink,
text.Rotherham: For I hungered, and ye gave me not to eat, [and] I was thirsty, and ye gave me not to drink,

text.NRSV: I was a foreigner and you did not welcome me, naked and you did not give me clothing, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.’
text.Rotherham: A stranger, was I, and ye took me not home, naked, and ye clothed me not, sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.

text.NRSV: Then they also will answer, ‘Lord, when was it that we saw you hungry or thirsty or a foreigner or naked or sick or in prison, and did not take care of you?’
text.Rotherham: Then, will, they also, answer, saying: Lord! when saw we thee—hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and ministered not unto thee?

text.NRSV: Then he will answer them, ‘Truly I tell you, just as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to me.’
text.Rotherham: Then, will he answer them, saying: Verily, I say unto you—Inasmuch as ye did it not, unto one of these least, neither unto me, did ye it.

text.NRSV: And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: And, these, shall go away, into, age-abiding, correction, but, the righteous, into, age-abiding, life.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended all these words, he said unto his disciples:

text.NRSV: “You know that after two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.”
text.Rotherham: Ye know that, after two days, the passover, taketh place,—and, the Son of Man, is to be delivered up, to be crucified.

text.NRSV: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people gathered in the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
text.Rotherham: Then, were gathered together, the High-priests and the Elders of the people, into the court of the High-priest who was called Caiaphas;

text.NRSV: and they conspired to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
text.Rotherham: and they took counsel together, in order that, Jesus, by guile, they might secure, and slay.

text.NRSV: But they said, “Not during the festival, or there may be a riot among the people.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying, however: Not during the feast, lest, an uproar, arise among the people.

text.NRSV: Now while Jesus was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper,
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, happening to be in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

text.NRSV: a woman came to him with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.
text.Rotherham: there came unto him a woman, holding an alabaster-jar of costly perfume, and she poured it down upon his head, as he was reclining.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples saw it, they were angry and said, “Why this waste?
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, seeing it, were greatly displeased, saying—To what end, this loss?

text.NRSV: For this ointment could have been sold for a large sum, and the money given to the poor.”
text.Rotherham: For this could have been sold for much, and given to the destitute.

text.NRSV: But Jesus, aware of this, said to them, “Why do you trouble the woman? She has performed a good service for me.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, taking note, said unto them—Why vex ye the woman? for, a seemly work, hath she wrought for me;

text.NRSV: For you always have the poor with you, but you will not always have me.
text.Rotherham: For, always, the destitute, have ye with you,—but, me, not always, have ye;

text.NRSV: By pouring this ointment on my body she has prepared me for burial.
text.Rotherham: For, she, pouring this perfume upon my body, so as to prepare me for burial, did it.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, wherever this good news is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Wheresoever this glad-message shall be proclaimed in all the world, also what she did, will be told, as a memorial of her.

text.NRSV: Then one of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
text.Rotherham: Then, went one of the twelve, the one called Judas Iscariot, unto the High-priests,

text.NRSV: and said, “What will you give me if I betray him to you?” They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
text.Rotherham: and said—What are ye willing to give unto me? and, I, unto you, will deliver him up. And they appointed him thirty pieces of silver.

text.NRSV: And from that moment he began to look for an opportunity to betray him.
text.Rotherham: And, from that time, was he seeking a favourable opportunity that he might, deliver him up.

text.NRSV: On the first day of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Where do you want us to make the preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
text.Rotherham: And, on the first of the days of unleavened bread, the disciples came unto Jesus, saying—Where wilt thou, that we make ready for thee to eat the passover?

text.NRSV: He said, “Go into the city to a certain man, and say to him, ‘The Teacher says, My time is near; I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.’”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Go your way into the city, unto such-a-one, and say to him, The teacher, saith, My season, is, near, with thee, will I keep the passover, with my disciples.

text.NRSV: So the disciples did as Jesus had directed them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And the disciples did, as Jesus directed them, and made ready the passover.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, he took his place with the twelve;
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, came, he was reclining with the twelve [disciples];

text.NRSV: and while they were eating, he said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me.”
text.Rotherham: and, as they were eating, he said—Verily, I say unto you, One from among you, will deliver me up.

text.NRSV: And they became greatly distressed and began to say to him one after another, “Surely not I, Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And, being exceedingly grieved, they began to be saying to him, each one—Can it be, I, Lord?

text.NRSV: He answered, “The one who has dipped his hand into the bowl with me will betray me.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—He who hath dipped with me his hand in the bowl, the same, will deliver me up.

text.NRSV: The Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that one by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that one not to have been born.”
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man, indeed, goeth his way, according as it is written concerning him,—But alas! for that man, through whom the Son of Man, is being delivered up: Well, had it been for him, if, that man, had not been born!

text.NRSV: Judas, who betrayed him, said, “Surely not I, Rabbi?” He replied, “You have said so.”
text.Rotherham: And Judas, who was delivering him up, answering, said—Can it be, I, Rabbi? He saith unto him—Thou, hast said.

text.NRSV: While they were eating, Jesus took a loaf of bread, and after blessing it he broke it, gave it to the disciples, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were eating, Jesus, taking a loaf and blessing, brake,—and, giving to his disciples, said—Take, eat! This, is, my body.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he gave it to them, saying, “Drink from it, all of you;
text.Rotherham: And, taking a cup, and giving thanks, he gave unto them, saying—Drink of it, all of you;

text.NRSV: for this is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: For, this, is my blood of the covenant, which, for many, is to be poured out, for remission of sins.

text.NRSV: I tell you, I will never again drink of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you—In nowise, will I drink, henceforth, of this produce of the vine, until that day, whensoever I shall drink it with you, new, in the kingdom of my Father.

text.NRSV: When they had sung the hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: And, having sung praise, they went forth unto the Mount of Olives.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “You will all become deserters because of me this night; for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus saith unto them—All ye, will find cause of stumbling in me, during this night; for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and, the sheep of the flock, will be scattered abroad;

text.NRSV: But after I am raised up, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
