text.NRSV: After the sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, late in the week, when it was on the point of dawning into the first of the week, came Mary the Magdalene, and the other Mary, to view the sepulchre.

text.NRSV: And suddenly there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord, descending from heaven, came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a great commotion occurred,—for, a messenger of the Lord, descending out of heaven, and coming near, had rolled away the stone, and was sitting upon it.

text.NRSV: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
text.Rotherham: Now his appearance, was, as lightning, and, his clothing, white as snow;

text.NRSV: For fear of him the guards shook and became like dead men.
text.Rotherham: and, for fear of him, they who were keeping watch were thrown into a commotion, and became as dead men.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid; I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
text.Rotherham: But the messenger, answering, said unto the women—Be not, ye, afraid! For I know that, Jesus the crucified, ye are seeking:

text.NRSV: He is not here; for he has been raised, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
text.Rotherham: He is not here, for he hath arisen, as he said. Come! see the place where he lay;

text.NRSV: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has been raised from the dead, and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him.’ This is my message for you.”
text.Rotherham: And, quickly going, tell his disciples—He hath arisen from the dead, and lo! he is going before you into Galilee;—There, shall ye yourselves see him: Lo! I have told you.

text.NRSV: So they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And, departing quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to bring tidings unto his disciples.

text.NRSV: Suddenly Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” And they came to him, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! Jesus, met them, saying—Joy to you! And, they, going forward, held his feet, and worshipped him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.”
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus saith unto them—Be not afraid! Go your way, bear tidings unto my brethren, that they may depart into Galilee; and, there, shall they themselves see me.

text.NRSV: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text.Rotherham: Now as they were, going, lo! certain of the guard, went into the city and reported unto the High-priests all the things that had come to pass;

text.NRSV: After the priests had assembled with the elders, they devised a plan to give a large sum of money to the soldiers,
text.Rotherham: and, being gathered together with the Elders and taking counsel, sufficient pieces of silver, gave they unto the soldiers,—

text.NRSV: telling them, “You must say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
text.Rotherham: saying—Say ye, His disciples, coming, by night, stole him, while we were sleeping;

text.NRSV: If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
text.Rotherham: And, if this be reported unto the governor, we, will persuade him, and will make, you, free from care.

text.NRSV: So they took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story is still told among the Jews to this day.
text.Rotherham: And, they, taking the pieces of silver, did as they were instructed. And this account was spread abroad among the Jews…until this [very] day.

text.NRSV: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.
text.Rotherham: And, the eleven disciples, went into Galilee, unto the mountain which, Jesus, had appointed them;

text.NRSV: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing him, they worshipped, but, some, doubted.

text.NRSV: And Jesus came and said to them, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, coming near, spake unto them, saying—All authority, in heaven and on earth, hath been given unto me;

text.NRSV: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
text.Rotherham: Go ye, therefore, and disciple all the nations, Immersing them into the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit,—

text.NRSV: and teaching them to obey everything that I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
text.Rotherham: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I myself have commanded you, And lo! I, am, with you, all the days, until the conclusion of the age.

17.1: And, after six days, Jesus taketh with him, Peter and James and John his brother, and bringeth them up into a high mountain, apart;
17.2: and was transformed before them,—and his face shone as the sun, and, his garments, became white as the light.
17.3: And lo! there appeared to them Moses and Elijah, conversing with him.
17.4: And Peter, answering, said unto Jesus—Lord! it is, delightful, for us, to be here,—If thou wilt, I will make here three tents, for thee, one, and, for Moses, one, and, for Elijah, one.
17.5: While yet he was speaking, lo! a brightly shining cloud, overshadowed them, and lo! a voice, out of the cloud, saying—This, is, my Son, the Beloved, in whom I delight,—Be hearkening to him.
17.6: And, hearing it, the disciples fell upon their face, and were caused to fear exceedingly.
17.7: And Jesus came near, and, touching them, said—Arise! and be not afraid.
17.8: And, lifting up their eyes, no one, saw they, save Jesus, himself, alone.
17.9: And, as they were coming down out of the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying—Unto no one, may ye tell the vision, until the Son of Man, from among the dead shall arise.
17.10: And the disciples questioned him, saying—Why then do, the Scribes, say, that, Elijah, must needs come, first?
17.11: And, he, answering, said—Elijah, indeed, cometh, and will restored all things;
17.12: But, I say unto you—that, Elijah, just now, came,—and they recognised him not, but did with him, whatsoever they pleased: Thus, the Son of Man also, is destined to suffer by them.
17.13: Then, perceived the disciples, that, concerning John the Immerser, he spake to them.
17.14: And, when they came unto the multitude, there approached him a man, falling on his knees to him,
17.15: and saying—Lord! have mercy upon my son,—because he is lunatic, and in a grievous condition; For, many times, falleth he into the fire, and, many times, into the water.
17.16: And I brought him unto thy disciples, and they could not cure, him.
17.17: And Jesus, answering, said—O faithless and perverted generation! How long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me, here.
17.18: And Jesus rebuked it; and the demon came out of him,—and cured was the boy, from that hour.
17.19: Then, the disciples, coming near, to Jesus, privately, said—Wherefore could, we, not cast it out?
17.20: And, he, saith unto them—Because of your little faith. For, verily, I say unto you—If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say to this mountain, remove hence yonder; and it shall be removed, and, nothing, shall be impossible to you.
17.22: And, as they were being gathered together in Galilee, Jesus said unto them—The Son of Man is about to be delivered up into the hands of men;
17.23: And they will slay him, and, on the third day, will he, arise. And they were grieved exceedingly.
17.24: And, when they came into Capernaum, they who, the half shekel, were receiving, came near unto Peter, and said, Your teacher, doth he not pay the half shekel?
17.25: He saith—Yea. And, coming into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, How, to thee, doth it seem, Simon? The kings of the earth, of whom receive they dues or tax? from their sons, or from the aliens?
17.26: And, when he said, From the aliens, Jesus said unto him, Well then, free, are the sons!
17.27: But, that we may not cause them to stumble, go unto the sea, and cast in a hook, and, the first fish that cometh up, take, and, opening its mouth, thou shalt find a shekel,—that, take, and give to them for me and thee.

18.1: In that hour came, the disciples unto Jesus, saying—Who then is, greatest, in the kingdom of the heavens?
18.2: And, calling near a child, he set it in the midst of them,
18.3: and said—Verily, I say unto you, Except ye turn and become as the children, in nowise, shall ye enter into the kingdom of the heavens.
18.4: Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this child, the same, is the greatest, in the kingdom of the heavens;
18.5: And, whosoever shall give welcome unto one such child as this, upon my name, unto me, giveth welcome.
18.6: And, whosoever shall cause to stumble one of these little ones who believe in me, it profiteth him, that there be hung a large mill-stone about his neck, and he be sunk in the wide main of the sea.
18.7: Alas for the world! by reason of the causes of stumbling; for it is, necessary, that the causes of stumbling come,—nevertheless, alas for the man through whom the cause of stumbling cometh!
18.8: But, if, thy hand or thy foot, be causing thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from thee: It is, seemly for thee, to enter into life, maimed or lame, rather than, having two hands or two feet, to be cast into the age-abiding fire.
18.9: And, if, thine eye, causeth thee to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: It is, seemly, for thee, one-eyed, into life, to enter, rather than, having two eyes, to be cast into the fiery gehenna.
18.10: Beware! do not despise one of these little ones; For I say unto you—that, their messengers in the heavens, do, continually, behold the face of my Father in the heavens.
18.12: How, to you, doth it seem? If a certain man come to have a hundred sheep and one from among them go astray, Will he not leave the ninety-nine upon the mountains, and going, seek the straying one?
18.13: And, if it should be that he find it, Verily, I say unto you—He rejoiceth over it, more than over the ninety-nine that have not gone astray.
18.14: Thus, there is no desire, in the presence of my Father who is in the heavens, that, one of these little ones, should be lost.
18.15: But, if thy brother sin, withdraw, convince him, betwixt thee and him, alone,—If unto thee he hearken, thou hast gained thy brother;
18.16: But, if he do not hearken, take with thee, yet one or two, that, at the mouth of two witnesses or three, every declaration, maybe established;
18.17: But, if he hear them amiss, tell it to the assembly,—And, if, even the assembly, he hear amiss, Let him be unto thee, just as the man of the nations and the tax-collector.
18.18: Verily, I say unto you—Whatsoever things ye shall bind on the earth, shall be bound in heaven; and, whatsoever things ye shall loose on the earth, shall be loosed in heaven.
18.19: Again, [verily] I say unto you—If two from among you shall agree upon the earth concerning any matter, whatsoever they shall ask, it shall be brought to pass for them, from my Father who is in the heavens;
18.20: For, where there are two or three, gathered together into my name, there, am I, in their midst.
18.21: Then, coming near, Peter said [to him]—Lord! how many times, shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? Until, seven, times?
18.22: Jesus saith to him—I say not unto thee, unto, seven, times, but, until seventy times seven.
18.23: For this cause, hath the kingdom of the heavens become like unto a man, a king, who wished to settle an account with his servants;
18.24: And, when he, began, to settle, there was brought unto him a, certain, debtor, of a thousand talents;
18.25: and, he, not having, wherewith to pay, the master ordered him to be sold, and the wife, and the children, and whatsoever he had,—and payment to be made.
18.26: The servant therefore, falling down, began to do homage unto him, saying—Have patience with me, and, all, will I pay thee.
18.27: And, moved with compassion, the master of that servant released him, and, the loan, he, forgave, him.
18.28: But that servant, going out, found one of his fellow-servants, who owed him, a hundred denaries, and, laying hold of him, he began seizing him by the throat, saying, Pay! if anything thou owest.
18.29: His fellow servant, therefore, falling down, began beseeching him, saying, Have patience with me! And I will pay thee.
18.30: He, however, would not, but went away and cast him into prison,—until he should pay what was owing.
18.31: His fellow-servants, therefore, seeing, the things that were done, were grieved exceedingly,—and went and made quite plain to their master all the things which had been done.
18.32: Then, calling him near, his master saith unto him—O wicked servant! All that debt, forgave I thee, because thou didst beseech me.
18.33: Was it not binding, upon thee also, to have mercy upon thy fellow-servant, as, I also, on thee, had mercy?
18.34: And, provoked to anger, his master delivered him up to the torturers, until he should pay all that was owing.
18.35: Thus, my heavenly Father also, will do unto you, if ye forgive not each one his brother, from your hearts.

23.1: Then, Jesus spake unto the multitudes and unto his disciples,
23.2: saying—Upon Moses’ seat, have sat down, the Scribes and the Pharisees:
23.3: All things, therefore, whatsoever they tell you, do and observe,—but, according to their works, do ye not, for they, say, and do not, perform.
23.4: But they bind together heavy burdens, and lay upon men’s shoulders, whereas, they, with their finger, are not willing to move them.
23.5: But, all their works, they do, to be gazed at, by men,—for they make broad their amulets, and make large their fringes,
23.6: And dearly love the first couch in the chief meals, and the first seats in the synagogues,
23.7: And the salutations in the market-places, and to be called by men, Rabbi.
23.8: But, ye,—do not be called Rabbi,—for, one, is your Teacher, and, all ye, are brethren;
23.9: And, father, be none of you called, upon the earth, for, one, is your Father, the Heavenly;
23.10: Neither be called leaders, for, your leader, is one, the Christ;
23.11: And, the greatest of you, shall be to you, a minister;
23.12: And, whoever shall exalt himself, shall be, abased, and, whoever shall abase himself, shall be, exalted,
23.14: But alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye are locking up the kingdom of the heavens before men,—for, ye, are not entering, neither, them who are entering, suffer ye to enter.
23.15: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: because ye compass sea and dry land, to make one convert—and, when it is done, ye make him a son of gehenna, twofold more than ye.
23.16: Alas for you, blind guides! that say—Whosoever shall swear by the Temple, it is, nothing, but, whosoever shall swear by the gold of the Temple, is bound:
23.17: Foolish and blind! for which is, greater, The gold, or the Temple that hath hallowed the gold?
23.18: And, whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is, nothing, but, whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it, is bound:
23.19: Blind! for which is greater, The gift, or the altar that halloweth the gift?
23.20: He therefore that hath sworn by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all that is upon it;
23.21: And, he that hath sworn by the Temple, sweareth by it, and by him who dwelleth therein;
23.22: And, he that hath sworn by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him who sitteth thereupon.
23.23: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye tithe the mint and the anise and the cummin,—and have dismissed the weightier matters of the law—the justice, the mercy, and the faith; whereas, these, it was binding, to do, and, those, not to, dismiss.
23.24: Blind guides! Straining out the gnat, but, the camel, swallowing.
23.25: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye cleanse the outside of the cup and of the dish,—while, within, they are full of plunder and intemperance.
23.26: Blind Pharisee! cleanse, first, the inside, of the cup [and of the dish], that, the outside thereof, may become, clean.
23.27: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye make yourselves like sepulchres whitewashed, which, outside, indeed, appear, beautiful, but, within, are full, of dead men’s bones and all uncleanness,—
23.28: Thus, ye also, outside, indeed, appear to men, righteous, but, within, are full, of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
23.29: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and adorn the monuments of the righteous,
23.30: and say—If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been their partners in the blood of the prophets:
23.31: So that ye bear witness against yourselves, that ye are, sons, of them who murdered the prophets.
23.32: And, ye, fill ye up the measure of your fathers!
23.33: Serpents! broods of vipers! how should ye flee from the judgment of gehenna?
23.34: For this cause, lo! I, send unto you, prophets and wise men and scribes,—some from among them, ye will slay and crucify, And some from among them ye will—scourge in your synagogues, and pursue from city to city:
23.35: That there may come upon you—all righteous blood poured out upon the earth, from the blood of Abel the righteous, unto the blood of Zachariah, son of Barachiah, whom ye murdered between the Temple and the altar.
23.36: Verily, I say unto you—All these things, will have come, upon this generation.
23.37: Jerusalem! Jerusalem! that slayeth the prophets, and stoneth them that have been sent unto her,—how often, would I have gathered thy children, like as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings,—and ye would not!
23.38: Lo! your house is left to you;
23.39: For I say unto you—in no wise, may ye see me, henceforth, until ye say, Blessed, is he that cometh, in the name of the Lord!
